FortSetting.AntiAliasingSuperResolutionFXAA_Descript: FXAA: Fast Approximate Anti-Aliasing requires less computing power, but being a spatial only anti-aliasing technique, it may result in flickering.
FortSetting.AntiAliasingSuperResolutionSMAA_Descript: SMAA: Subpixel Morphological Anti-Aliasing is more expensive than FXAA but has still low computing power requirements. Being a spatial only anti-aliasing technique, it may result in flickering.
FortSetting.AntiAliasingSuperResolutionTAA_Descripti: TAA: Temporal Anti-Aliasing combines previous frames with the current frame to provide a better quality than FXAA.
FortSetting.AntiAliasingSuperResolutionTSR_Descripti: TSR Low/Medium/High/Epic: Temporal Super Resolution improves anti-aliasing quality, and overall rendering performance, by rendering fewer pixels each frame to produce comparable visual quality output image.
FortSetting.TextChatAutomatedReport_Disclaimer: In all Game Channels and all text chats that include players under the age of 18, our systems will automatically detect and report threats of real world violence, child endangerment, and self-harm to Epic for review.
FortSetting.LumenRequiresNanite: 'Nanite Virtualized Geometry' must be set to 'On' in order to use Lumen
FortSetting.ResolutionScale_Description:
3D Resolution determines the resolution that objects are rendered in game. Lower resolutions can significantly increase frame rate.

If Dynamic 3D Resolution is enabled, resolution may increase from the specified value if the GPU load can still fit within the specified Frame Rate Limit.
FortSetting.MobileResolutionToggle_Description:
3D resolution determines the resolution that objects are rendered in game.

Use <strong>AUTO</> to allow the game to dynamically adjust the 3D resolution based on your device performance while you play.

Use <strong>CUSTOM</> to manually set a static 3D resolution.
FortSetting.Mobile_ResolutionScale_Description: 3D resolution determines the resolution that objects are rendered in game. Lower resolutions can significantly increase frame rate.
FortSetting.PartyRiftRequestToJoin_Description:
<Icon.Warning></> If your Party Privacy Setting is set to Private - this will need to be turned on in order to use Party Signal.

On - allows anyone to send you a request to join.
Off - only allows friends to send you a request to join.
FortSetting.TouchInteractMode_Buttons_Description: <strong>Easy Interact</>: Large representative icons for interactions and loot pick ups.
FortSetting.TouchInteractMode_InWorld_Description: <strong>In World</>: Interaction icons displayed in 3D space near their interactions.
FortSetting.DownloadMethod_Description:
<strong>ON:</> When Wi-Fi is not available, downloads will continue using cellular data.

<strong>OFF:</> Fewer downloads may occur over cellular data. You will be asked to confirm or start downloads manually.
FortSetting.CanAppearOnCareerLeaderboard_Description:
<strong>ON</>: Others will be able to get your current gameplay stats

<strong>OFF</>: Others WILL NOT be able to get your current gameplay stats
FortSetting.CanReceiveGifts_Description:
<strong>ON</>: You will be able to receive gifts from friends

<strong>OFF</>: You WILL NOT be able to receive gifts from friends

NOTE: You need to be friends with the other player for at least 48 hours before receiving gifts.
FortSetting.SimpleBuild_Description:
<strong>Simple Build</> reduces the number of buttons required to build in Fortnite.

<strong>Primary Button</> gives you access to Stairs, Floors, and Roof on one button. Look up or down to build stairs, forward to build floors.

<strong>Secondary Button</> gives you immediate access to Walls. Protect yourself easily with a press of a button.
FortSetting.AmpleHammer_SettingDescription:
<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">ON</>: Your child can interact with AI features in Epic Games products through voice and free-text written communication. This includes interacting with conversational AI non-playable characters in Fortnite

<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">OFF</>: Your child cannot interact with AI features in Epic Games' products through voice and written communication.

Note: This control does not apply to AI-powered support interactions.
FortSetting.AllowPaidRandomItemsSetting_Description:
<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">On</>: Your child CAN spend V-Bucks (or content purchased with V-Bucks) on items or content where the reward is random. 

<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">Off</>: Your child CANNOT spend V-Bucks (or content purchased with V-Bucks) on items or content where the reward is random.
FortSetting.AllowCreativeExperiences_Desc:
<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">On</>: Your child can design games and experiences in Fortnite, and can join their friends while they design their own experiences.

<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">Off</>: Your child cannot design games and experiences in Fortnite, and cannot join their friends while they design their own experiences.

Note: While designing a Fortnite experience, players can interact with content that has not been submitted to Fortnite Publishing for moderation and thus has not been rated. Players may also create content, including text, which may be visible to their collaborators. 

Learn more about Creative at fn.gg/creative.
FortSetting.PredownloadCosmetics_InstallSize: <text color="FFFF00">Enabling Pre-Download Cosmetic Textures will use {InstallSize} more storage capacity.</>
FortSetting.AnnualRefundTokenRequests_Description:
<text size="22">Cancel Purchase</>

You may cancel any unintentional purchase made with V-Bucks. Cancel Purchase is available until you equip or use your purchase during gameplay. Only items purchased within the last 24 hours are eligible for Cancel Purchase.

For more info see fn.gg/cancelpurchase

<text size="24">Returning Purchases</>

In cases where you unintentionally purchase an item with V-Bucks and purchase cancelation is no longer available, you can use one of your 3 annual Return Tickets instead. You’ll receive one additional Return Ticket every 12 months, up to a maximum of 3. Each ticket is added 12 months following the most recent ticket you received.

Only items purchased within the last 30 days are eligible for returns. Consumable items - such as the Battle Pass, Battle Pass Levels, or Llamas - are not returnable.
FortSetting.ScopedSpeedMultiplier_Description: A multiplier that is applied to the look sensitivity when aiming down sights with scoped weapons.  Settings less than 1x will decrease sensitivity when aiming down scope.  Settings greater than 1x will increase sensitivity when aiming down scope.
FortSetting.LookBuildModeMultiplier_Description: A multiplier that is applied to the look sensitivity when in build mode.  Settings less than 1x will decrease sensitivity when in build mode.  Settings greater than 1x will increase sensitivity when in build mode.
FortSetting.LookEditModeMultiplier_Description: A multiplier that is applied to the look sensitivity when in edit mode.  Settings less than 1x will decrease sensitivity when in edit mode.  Settings greater than 1x will increase sensitivity when in edit mode.
FortSetting.BuildingPossession_Description: A quick glance at a building will show if it can be edited.
FortSetting.UseLatencyFlash_Description: A white box will appear when clicking the left mouse button to assist measuring system latency. Requires an NVIDIA video card and the latest drivers.
FortSetting.GyroV2Acceleration_Description:
Acceleration lets you increase your aim sensitivity when turning the controller quickly.

Acceleration can give you a wider range of movement while still keeping your sensitivity low when you want to keep your aim steady. Acceleration is capped so that your fast movements are still consistent and predictable. Selecting CUSTOM will give you more control over the acceleration cap and where it's applied.

The LEGACY option is for those who want their gyro acceleration to feel the same as it did in older versions.
FortSetting.UserInterfaceContrast_Description: Adjust the contrast of the user interface.
FortSetting.SteadyingGreyedOut: Advanced Gyro Options are enabled, so this setting is overridden by the Advanced Gyro Filters below.
FortSetting.AimAssistGreyedOut: Aim Assist is unavailable while Gyro Aiming is active.
FortSetting.AimAssistPullStrength_Description: Aim assist helps you match the speed of your aim to the speed of targets you're aiming at.  Lowering this setting will reduce this effect, and a setting of 0% will disable this aspect of aim assist.  Lowering this setting is not recommended.
FortSetting.AimAssistSlowStrength_Description: Aim assist slows your aim near targets to help you make precise adjustments.  Lowering this setting will reduce this effect, and a setting of 0% will disable this aspect of aim assist.  Lowering this setting is not recommended.
FortSetting.DisableSetting_GameCacheDeleting: All game assets marked for removal and will be uninstalled the next time Fortnite is relaunched.
FortSetting.AllowCreativeExperiences_Name: Allow Creative Mode And Unreal Editor
FortSetting.AllowDiscoveryPersonalization_Name: Allow Personalization of Island and Creator Recommendations
FortSetting.AllowNotifications_Description: Allow notifications and get notifications for content downloads, friend and party invites, new seasons, limited time events, announcements and promotions. Configure these settings at any time.
FortSetting.AmpleHammer_DetailName: Allow voice and written communication with AI Features
FortSetting.AutoDownloadHighResTexturesReminders_Des:
Allows reminders to be shown regarding turning automatic downloads of high resolution textures back on.

NOTE: Reminders only appear when you have the required hard drive space to enable this feature and automatic downloads are turned 'OFF'.

OFF: Fortnite will not send reminders about enabling high resolution textures.

ON (default): Fortnite will send reminders about enabling high resolution textures.
FortSetting.AllowLowPower_Description: Allows the game to automatically reduce settings to preserve power.
FortSetting.LockPrimaryInputMethodToMouse_Descriptio: Allows using Controller Input and Mouse Input together, and locks the input feedback to Mouse and Keyboard suggestions, rather than having the input methods fight.
FortSetting.EnablePreferredItemSlots_Description:
Allows you to assign a preferred inventory slot for a weapon or item type. On pick up, your preferred item will be placed into its preferred inventory slot.

Unassigned items will be placed in the first available quickbar slot and rearranged if a preferred item type is picked up. 

• Rarity swapping will occur when picking up a higher rarity like-for-like item. Eg. Uncommon Pump Shotgun will swap for an Epic Pump Shotgun. The lower rarity item will be relocated to the first available slot.

• Auto-Sort Consumables to Right will continue to work with Preferred Item Slots.

• Preferred item slots will only be active when you have at least one empty inventory slot.
FortSetting.KBMBindingPreset_Description: Allows you to reset the bindings to the keys to a new preset.
FortSetting.ControllerBindingPreset_Description:
Allows you to select which controller preset to use.

<strong>Old School</>: Original controls for Fortnite.

<strong>Quick Builder</>: A preset that focuses on quick building.

<strong>Combat Pro</>: A preset that focuses on combat.

<strong>Builder Pro (Default)</>: A preset that focuses on building. <strong>This is the recommended default for Fortnite.</>

<strong>Custom</>: Custom controls set by the mappings below.

FortSetting.AntiAliasingSuperResolution_DescriptionH:
Anti-Aliasing smooths jagged edges and can stabilize the image under motion. Super Resolution methods do the same while additionally improving performance by rendering fewer pixels each frame to generate the same resolution output image.

Off: Anti-Aliasing is turned off.
FortSetting.SqueegeeUnlockedContentDescription: Any content you've unlocked for your child can be viewed and locked here.
FortSetting.GyroSmoothingWindow_Description: Any gyro input below the Smoothing Threshold will be averaged out over this amount of time. A larger window means smoother but more laggy movements.
FortSetting.AutoConfirmEdits_Description: Are edit actions automatically confirmed and applied when you finish them? EDIT refers to changing the shape of a piece, RESET refers to returning a piece to its original shape, and BOTH will automatically confirm and apply both EDIT actions and RESET actions.
FortSetting.LogoutBody: Are you sure you want to log out of Fortnite?
FortSetting.ArmASR is Disabled: Arm ASR is disabled in the Anti Aliasing & Super Resolution setting.
FortSetting.ArmASR_Description: Arm ASR: Arm® Accuracy Super Resolution™ temporal upscaling can improve performance by simplifying the rendering process with a limited impact on quality.
FortSetting.ArmASR is Enabled: ArmASR is enabled. Use the Arm ASR setting to control the 3D resolution rendered every frame. Use 'Custom' for fine control.
FortSetting.PlaytimeReportingFrequency_Description:
As an optional service, a weekly playtime report can be generated and sent to the email associated with this account.

<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">Note</>: Click on "More Settings" below to learn about playtime restrictions on your platform.
<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">On</>: A weekly report will be sent to the email address associated with this account.
<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">Off</>: No reports will be sent.
FortSetting.PreferredItemSlotIndex_Desc:
Assign a weapon or item type to Slot {0} of your inventory.

A common use for Slot {1}: {2}.

• Rarity swapping will occur when picking up a higher rarity like-for-like item. Eg. Uncommon Pump Shotgun will swap for an Epic Pump Shotgun. The lower rarity item will be relocated to the first available slot.

• Auto-Sort Consumables to Right will continue to work

• Preferred item slots will only be active until you have a complete loadout.
FortSetting.VisualizeAudioSourcesPriority_Descriptio:
Audio visualizer priority: 
All - Shows all indicators 
Critical - Shows Footsteps/Vehicles, Gunshots, and Healing indicators
FortSetting.AutoSortConsumablesToHigherSlots_Name: Auto Sort Consumables to Higher Slots
FortSetting.DynamicResolution_Description: Automatically adjusts the 3D resolution based on GPU load. When GPU usage is low, the resolution increases up to native resolution. When GPU usage is high, the resolution decreases down to the Minimum 3D Resolution setting.
FortSetting.AutoSetQuality_Description: Automatically configure the graphics quality options based on a benchmark of the hardware.
FortSetting.EditConfirmOnRelease_Description_Switch: Automatically confirms an edit action on Button released.
FortSetting.EditConfirmOnRelease_Description_PS4_PS5: Automatically confirms an edit action on button released.
FortSetting.EditConfirmOnRelease_Description: Automatically confirms an edit action on input released.
FortSetting.ResetConfirmOnRelease_Description_Switch: Automatically confirms an edit reset action on Button released.
FortSetting.ResetConfirmOnRelease_Description_PS4_PS: Automatically confirms an edit reset action on button released.
FortSetting.ResetConfirmOnRelease_Description: Automatically confirms an edit reset action on input released.
FortSetting.AutomaticallyApplyXP_Description: Automatically level up Heroes and Defenders in your active Hero Loadout, and Survivors in your Squads, from your available People XP
FortSetting.AutoMulchMode_Traps_Description: Automatically recycle Trap schematics at this rarity or below.
FortSetting.AutoMulchMode_Weapons_Description: Automatically recycle Weapon schematics at this rarity or below.
FortSetting.AutoMulchMode_Defenders_Description: Automatically retire Defenders at this rarity or below.
FortSetting.AutoMulchMode_Heroes_Description: Automatically retire Heroes at this rarity or below.
FortSetting.AutoMulchMode_Survivors_Description: Automatically retire Survivors at this rarity or below.
FortSetting.OnlyPrimaryPlayerEditable: Can only be changed by the primary player.
FortSetting.CanOnlyBeChangedInFrontend: Cannot be changed while playing a match.
FortSetting.3DAudioDisabled_VisSFX: Cannot be used with Visualize Sound Effects.
FortSetting.MotionWarning: Caution! Enabling the gyroscope could drain your battery faster.
FortSetting.ReticleColorChangeForEnemy_Description: Change the color of reticle when enemy in range
FortSetting.GyroButtonX_Description: Changes the X-axis behaviour of holding the Gyro Modifier button if you have set one on your controller.
FortSetting.GyroButtonY_Description: Changes the Y-axis behaviour of holding the Gyro Modifier button if you have set one on your controller.
FortSetting.VoiceChatInputDevice_Description: Changes the voice chat input device, the device with a microphone that will receive your voice chat that teammates will hear.
FortSetting.VoiceChatOutputDevice_Description: Changes the voice chat output device, where you will hear your teammates that are using voice chat.
FortSetting.OpenBlog_Description: Check out the official Fortnite website to help you get the most out of the Video settings.
FortSetting.SubtitleBackgroundOpacity_Description: Choose a different background or letterboxing for the subtitles.
FortSetting.SubtitleTextBorder_Description: Choose different borders for the text.
FortSetting.SubtitleTextColor_Description: Choose different colors for the subtitle text.
FortSetting.SubtitleTextSize_Description: Choose different sizes of the the subtitle text.
FortSetting.InstalledTextureQuality_Description:
Choose the level of detail for Fortnite’s textures. Increasing this setting will make Fortnite stream in textures as they are encountered in-game and look more detailed, but may increase network usage and decrease performance on your device.

<text color="FFFF00">’Cellular Data Download’ must be set to ‘ON’ in order to use higher quality textures over cellular data.</>
FortSetting.Hitmarkers_Description: Choose which sounds play when hitting a target.
FortSetting.TextChat_Desc:
Choose who your child can send and receive messages with in groups, parties, and games. Your child can only send and receive one-on-one messages with Epic friends.

<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">Everybody</>: Your child can text chat with everyone.

<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">Friends, team members, and connections</>: Your child can text chat with Epic friends, platform friends{0}, everyone on their team, and connections. Connections are people who your child isn't friends with but who are connected to your child through their friends. Team sizes vary by experience and can range from a few players to everyone in a game.

<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">Friends Only</>: Your child can text chat only with their Epic and platform friends.

<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">Nobody</>: Your child can't text chat with anyone.

Selecting <strong>Nobody</> still allows players to use messages from a list of in-game safe phrases (like "Nice one!"), if supported.
FortSetting.VoiceChat_ParentalControls_Description_E:
Choose who your child can speak with in groups, parties, and games.

<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">Everybody</> : Your child can voice chat with everyone.

<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">Friends, team members, and connections</> : Your child can voice chat with Epic friends, platform friends{0}, everyone on their team, and connections. Connections are people who your child isn't friends with but who are connected to your child through their friends. Team sizes vary by experience and can range from a few players to everyone in a game.

<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">Friends only</> : Your child can voice chat only with their Epic and platform friends.

<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">Nobody</> : Your child can't voice chat with anyone.
FortSetting.SubtitlePage_Description: Configure the visual appearance of subtitles.
FortSetting.AutoHurdle_Description:
Control how your player hurdle.

Hold Forward - Automatically hurdle over small obstacle.
Press Jump - Manually start hurdle over obstacle when pressing jump.
FortSetting.AutoClamber_Description:
Control how your player mantles and grabs onto ledges.

Hold Forward in Air - Jump and Hold Forward to grab onto a nearby ledge.
Hold Jump in Air - Jump and Hold jump to grab onto a nearby ledge.
FortSetting.SocialPrivacy_MutualFriends_Description:
Controls visibility of your mutual friends to other players.

<strong>PRIVATE</>: No one can see your mutual friends.

<strong>FRIENDS ONLY</>: Only friends can see your mutual friends.

<strong>PUBLIC</>: Anyone can see your mutual friends.
FortSetting.HiddenMatchmakingDelay_Description: Creates a random small delay during matchmaking and hides matchmaking status updates to limit repeat in-game harassment. The random delay is chosen between 0 and the number of seconds selected. Each attempt to play will randomly generate a delay. NOTE: Enabling this setting will cause longer queue times.

Each attempt to play will randomly generate a delay. NOTE: Enabling this setting will cause longer queue times.

FortSetting.SetupAdvancedToMatchLegacy_Description: Creates an approximation of the legacy controller settings using the new advanced options.
FortSetting.EnableCustomKeyboardDiagonals_Descriptio: Customize the direction of your diagonal movement when moving with keyboard controls.
FortSetting.LookInputCurve_Description_Switch: Defines how input from the Right Stick is processed.  Linear uses the raw Stick input.  Exponential causes smaller Stick movement to have less of an effect, allowing for more precision.
FortSetting.LookInputCurve_Description_Switch2: Defines how input from the Right Stick is processed.  Linear uses the raw Stick input.  Exponential causes smaller Stick movement to have less of an effect, allowing for more precision.
FortSetting.LookInputCurve_Description: Defines how input from the right stick is processed.  Linear uses the raw stick input.  Exponential causes smaller stick movement to have less of an effect, allowing for more precision.
FortSetting.LookInputCurve_Description_PS4: Defines how input from the right stick is processed.  Linear uses the raw stick input.  Exponential causes smaller stick movement to have less of an effect, allowing for more precision.
FortSetting.LookInputCurve_Description_PS5: Defines how input from the right stick is processed.  Linear uses the raw stick input.  Exponential causes smaller stick movement to have less of an effect, allowing for more precision.
FortSetting.LookInputCurve_Description_XB1: Defines how input from the right stick is processed.  Linear uses the raw stick input.  Exponential causes smaller stick movement to have less of an effect, allowing for more precision.
FortSetting.LookInputCurve_Description_XSX: Defines how input from the right stick is processed.  Linear uses the raw stick input.  Exponential causes smaller stick movement to have less of an effect, allowing for more precision.
FortSetting.GlobalMaxAspectRatio_Description:
Determines how the HUD adapts to ultra-wide monitors.

Expand: Expands the HUD to match your ultra-wide monitor. This results in the HUD displaying towards the edges of the screen.

Clamp: Keeps all HUD elements positioned within a standard 16:9 safe area while still displaying gameplay in ultra-wide.
FortSetting.VoiceChatMethod_Description_ToggleVoice:
Determines whether your microphone is an <strong>open mic</>, or requires a <strong>toggle button</> to activate/deactivate.

<strong>OPEN MIC:</> Your microphone is always on and your teammates can hear you at all times.

<strong>TOGGLE VOICE:</> Other players will only hear you talking when you activate your microphone by tapping the "Toggle Voice" button.
FortSetting.VoiceChatMethod_Description_Controller:
Determines whether your microphone is an <strong>open mic</>, or requires a button <strong>push to talk</>.

<strong>OPEN MIC:</> Your microphone is always on and your teammates can hear you at all times.

<strong>PUSH TO TALK:</> Your microphone is only on and teammates will only hear you talking when you press and hold the 'push to talk' button. The push to talk button can be set in the Controls tab.
FortSetting.VoiceChatMethod_Description_KeyboardCont:
Determines whether your microphone is an <strong>open mic</>, or requires a button <strong>push to talk</>.

<strong>OPEN MIC:</> Your microphone is always on and your teammates can hear you at all times.

<strong>PUSH TO TALK:</> Your microphone is only on and teammates will only hear you talking when you press and hold the 'push to talk' button. The push to talk button can be set in the Keyboard Controls tab.
FortSetting.ControllerPlatform_Description: Determines which controller platform to use when displaying bindings.
FortSetting.AllowedConfrontationalEmoteSources_Descr:
Determines which players you will see overly confrontational emotes from.

<strong>FROM ANYONE:</> You will see these emotes from all players.

<strong>FROM FRIENDS IN PARTY:</> You will only see these emotes from friends in your party.

<strong>NEVER:</> You will not see these emotes from other players.
<strong>Emotes:</>
	<strong>•</> Laugh It Up
	<strong>•</> Take the L
	<strong>•</> Whip Crack
	<strong>•</> Make It Plantain
	<strong>•</> Trophy Drop
	<strong>•</> Feel It
FortSetting.GamepadVibrationBlocksDevice_Description: Disables device vibration when a controller is attached.
FortSetting.GyroDisabledWhileDriving_Description: Disables gyro aiming while in the driver's seat of a vehicle.
FortSetting.Resolution_Description: Display Resolution determines the size of the window in Windowed mode. In Fullscreen mode, Display Resolution determines the graphics card output resolution, which can result in black bars depending on monitor and graphics card. Display Resolution is inactive in Windowed Fullscreen mode.
FortSetting.CosmeticStreaming_Description:
Download only the necessary cosmetics when you encounter them while playing.

NOTE: Slower internet connections may experience performance issues when the game is streaming cosmetics. Turning this feature off will require more disk space.

ON: Fortnite will only download cosmetics encountered in-game.

OFF: Fortnite will download all cosmetics.
FortSetting.CanSeeOtherPlayersNames_Desc:
During a match, the account names of players who are eliminated will appear on the screen.
This setting allows you to replace the names of other players who are not in your squad with "Player" in those messages.

<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">Note</>: You will still be able to see the names of people in the same "squad" (up to three other players on your team who may or may not be friends).
<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">On</>: You will see the names of players that are not squad members.
<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">Off</>: Replaces the names of players that are not in your squad with "Player"
FortSetting.CanDisplayMyUserName_Desc:
During a match, the account names of players who are eliminated will appear on the screen.
This setting allows you to replace your name with "Anonymous" in these messages for everyone who is not in your squad.

<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">Note</>: People in the same "squad" (up to three other players on your team who may or may not be friends) will still be able to see your account name.
<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">On</>: Players that are not in your squad will be able to see your account name.
<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">Off</>: Replace your name to players that are not in your squad with "Anonymous".
FortSetting.ShowSeasonLevel_Description:
During a match, your account name will show your Season level in the Elimination Feed.

This setting allows you to hide your Season level in those messages.

ON: Shows your Season level to players that are not in your squad.

OFF: Your Season level will be hidden for players that are not squad members.
FortSetting.DynamicResOnlyWithTSR: Dynamic resolution is only available with TSR in the Anti-Aliasing and Super-Resolution settings.
FortSetting.DynamicResOnlyWithD3D12: Dynamic-resolution is only available when DirectX 12 is supported.
FortSetting.DynamicResOnlyWithBelowNative: Dynamic-resolution is only available when setting a 3D resolution lower than 100%.
FortSetting.DynamicResOnlyWithFrameRateLimit: Dynamic-resolution is only available when setting a Frame Rate Limit.
FortSetting.VisualEffectQuality_Description: Effects determines the quality of visual effects in game. Increasing this setting will increase the quality of visual effects, but can reduce performance.
FortSetting.HeadphoneMode_Description: Enable binaural audio.  Provides 3D audio spatialization, so you can hear the location of sounds more precisely, including above, below, and behind you. Recommended for use with stereo headphones only.
FortSetting.QuickWeaponToggle_Description_Switch:
Enable the experimental Quick Weapon action. When enabled, Quick Weapon replaces the Next Weapon button.

Press Quick Weapon to equip your primary item. Press one of the A/B/X/Y buttons while pressing Quick Weapon to switch to one of the other item slots. You can press Quick Weapon and A/B/X/Y simultaneously to swap to that item instantly, or hold the Quick Weapon button for as long as you like to get used to how the A/B/X/Y buttons match up with the items in your inventory.

When this is enabled, Previous Weapon is replaced by Place Marker to make it easier to communicate with your squad. Or you can replace Previous Weapon with the action of your choice by customizing your controls in the Controller Customization tab.
FortSetting.QuickWeaponToggle_Description_Switch2:
Enable the experimental Quick Weapon action. When enabled, Quick Weapon replaces the Next Weapon button.

Press Quick Weapon to equip your primary item. Press one of the A/B/X/Y buttons while pressing Quick Weapon to switch to one of the other item slots. You can press Quick Weapon and A/B/X/Y simultaneously to swap to that item instantly, or hold the Quick Weapon button for as long as you like to get used to how the A/B/X/Y buttons match up with the items in your inventory.

When this is enabled, Previous Weapon is replaced by Place Marker to make it easier to communicate with your squad. Or you can replace Previous Weapon with the action of your choice by customizing your controls in the Controller Customization tab.
FortSetting.QuickWeaponToggle_Description:
Enable the experimental Quick Weapon action. When enabled, Quick Weapon replaces the Next Weapon button.

Press Quick Weapon to equip your primary item. Press one of the action buttons while pressing Quick Weapon to switch to one of the other item slots. You can press Quick Weapon and an action button simultaneously to swap to that item instantly, or hold the Quick Weapon button for as long as you like to get used to how the action buttons match up with the items in your inventory.

When this is enabled, Previous Weapon is replaced by Place Marker to make it easier to communicate with your squad. Or you can replace Previous Weapon with the action of your choice by customizing your controls in the Controller Customization tab.
FortSetting.QuickWeaponToggle_Description_PS4:
Enable the experimental Quick Weapon action. When enabled, Quick Weapon replaces the Next Weapon button.

Press Quick Weapon to equip your primary item. Press one of the action buttons while pressing Quick Weapon to switch to one of the other item slots. You can press Quick Weapon and an action button simultaneously to swap to that item instantly, or hold the Quick Weapon button for as long as you like to get used to how the action buttons match up with the items in your inventory.

When this is enabled, Previous Weapon is replaced by Place Marker to make it easier to communicate with your squad. Or you can replace Previous Weapon with the action of your choice by customizing your controls in the Controller Customization tab.

FortSetting.InstantBoostWhenBuilding_Description_Swi: Enable this setting to ignore the boost ramp time when in build mode.  The boost speed will engage instantly when the Stick is fully deflected when in build mode.
Enable this setting to ignore the boost ramp time when in build mode.  The boost speed will engage instantly when the Stick is fully deflected when in build mode.
FortSetting.InstantBoostWhenBuilding_Description: Enable this setting to ignore the boost ramp time when in build mode.  The boost speed will engage instantly when the stick is fully deflected when in build mode.
FortSetting.InstantBoostWhenBuilding_Description_PS4: Enable this setting to ignore the boost ramp time when in build mode.  The boost speed will engage instantly when the stick is fully deflected when in build mode.
FortSetting.InstantBoostWhenBuilding_Description_PS5: Enable this setting to ignore the boost ramp time when in build mode.  The boost speed will engage instantly when the stick is fully deflected when in build mode.

FortSetting.InstantBoostWhenBuilding_Description_XSX: Enable this setting to ignore the boost ramp time when in build mode.  The boost speed will engage instantly when the stick is fully deflected when in build mode.
FortSetting.120fps_Description: Enable this to run the game at 120fps.
FortSetting.120fps_Description_Playstation: Enable this to run the game at 120fps. Please note that depending on your TV capabilities, your display resolution could be reduced to support this mode.
FortSetting.ShowFPS_Description: Enable this to show the frames per second. Use this to help gauge the effect of adjusting different video settings.
FortSetting.UseLegacyControls_Description: Enable to revert back to the legacy sensitivity behavior.
FortSetting.ShowVoiceIndicators_Description: Enables / Disables the notifications that appear when someone in your voice channel speaks.
FortSetting.GamepadFlickStick_Description_Switch:
Enables Flick Stick. When enabled, point the Right Stick on your controller in any direction to quickly flick the camera to face that direction. For example, pulling the Stick back will flick behind you. Once the Stick is tilted, rotate it to adjust your bearing.

Since this means you can no longer aim up and down with the Stick, it's best to have Gyro Aiming always active or set Right Stick Effect to ENABLES so that you can activate gyro to look up or down at any time by engaging the Stick.
FortSetting.GamepadFlickStick_Description_Switch2:

Since this means you can no longer aim up and down with the Stick, it's best to have Gyro Aiming always active or set Right Stick Effect to ENABLES so that you can activate gyro to look up or down at any time by engaging the Stick.

FortSetting.AutoDownloadHighResTextures_Description:
Enables Fortnite to automatically download high resolution textures. Higher resolution textures make Fortnite look more detailed.

NOTE: Slower internet connections or devices with limited hard drive space may experience performance issues when the game is downloading high resolution textures. Turning this feature off may help improve performance and save disk space.

ON: Fortnite will automatically download high resolution textures.

OFF: Fortnite will not automatically download high resolution textures.
FortSetting.EasyLootGridEnabled_Description: Enables a scrollable widget where nearby items are shown. You can then pickup an item from there by clicking on it rather than having to click on the actual item widget on the scene.
FortSetting.EnergySavingIdle_Description: Enables energy savings when the game goes into idle mode in the FrontEnd. Idle mode starts after 30 seconds of inactivity.
FortSetting.EnergySavingFocus_Description: Enables energy savings when the game window is not the active window.
FortSetting.GyroV2AdvancedEnabled_Description: Enables or Disables Advanced Gyro Options.
FortSetting.GyroV2Enabled_Description_Switch: Enables or Disables Gyro Aiming. When active, turn your controller to turn the camera. This offers more precision than Right Stick aiming, but doesn't have to replace it entirely. Usually players will still use the Right Stick for most camera control while using gyro aiming for finer adjustments that are difficult to do with a stick.
FortSetting.GyroV2Enabled_Description_Switch2: Enables or Disables Gyro Aiming. When active, turn your controller to turn the camera. This offers more precision than Right Stick aiming, but doesn't have to replace it entirely. Usually players will still use the Right Stick for most camera control while using gyro aiming for finer adjustments that are difficult to do with a stick.
FortSetting.GyroV2Enabled_Description_PS4: Enables or Disables Gyro Aiming. When active, turn your controller to turn the camera. This offers more precision than right stick aiming, but doesn't have to replace it entirely. Usually players will still use the right stick for most camera control while using gyro aiming for finer adjustments that are difficult to do with a stick.
FortSetting.GyroV2Enabled_Description_PS5: Enables or Disables Gyro Aiming. When active, turn your controller to turn the camera. This offers more precision than right stick aiming, but doesn't have to replace it entirely. Usually players will still use the right stick for most camera control while using gyro aiming for finer adjustments that are difficult to do with a stick.
FortSetting.GyroV2Enabled_Description: Enables or Disables Gyro Aiming. When active, turn your controller to turn the camera. This offers more precision than standard stick aiming, but doesn't have to replace it entirely. Usually players will still use the right stick for most camera control while using gyro aiming for finer adjustments that are difficult to do with a stick.

FortSetting.PlayerSurveys_Description: Enables or disables whether or not you receive periodic player surveys from Epic Games in game. Player surveys are optional and help guide the development of Fortnite.
FortSetting.Vsync_Description: Enabling Vsync eliminates screen tearing by always rendering and presenting a full frame. Disabling Vsync can give higher frame rate and better input response, but can result in horizontal screen tearing.
FortSetting.CameraAssists_Description: Enabling camera assists means the camera will actively turn as you move your character around, so that you don't have to manually control the camera unless trying to aim at something.
FortSetting.AutoCenter_Description: Enabling this setting will pull the camera towards the horizon when you're moving about and not aiming or firing. This can help new players who find themselves unintentionally staring at the ground or sky, or flick stick players who only have gyro aiming active in certain aim modes.
FortSetting.ExperimentalWarning: Experimental feature for this game mode.
FortSetting.WarningAboutEnableFlickStick_Message_Swi:
Flick Stick can only turn the camera left and right. When Flick Stick is enabled, you can only look up and down using gyro controls.

In order to be able to look up and down at all times, we strongly recommend Flick Stick players set their Gyro Active Modes to ALWAYS or set Right Stick Effect to ENABLES. This lets you activate gyro aiming at any time by engaging the Flick Stick.
FortSetting.WarningAboutEnableFlickStick_Message:
Flick Stick can only turn the camera left and right. When Flick Stick is enabled, you can only look up and down using gyro controls.

In order to be able to look up and down at all times, we strongly recommend Flick Stick players set their Gyro Active Modes to ALWAYS or set right stick Effect to ENABLES. This lets you activate gyro aiming at any time by engaging the Flick Stick.
FortSetting.WarningAboutEnableFlickStick_Message_PS4:
Flick Stick can only turn the camera left and right. When Flick Stick is enabled, you can only look up and down using gyro controls.

In order to be able to look up and down at all times, we strongly recommend Flick Stick players set their Gyro Active Modes to ALWAYS or set right stick Effect to ENABLES. This lets you activate gyro aiming at any time by engaging the Flick Stick.
FortSetting.WarningAboutEnableFlickStick_MessageMobi:
Flick Stick requires playing with a controller. Since Flick Stick also requires your mobile device's gyro for gyro aiming, enabling Flick Stick on mobile is only recommended for players who can mount their mobile device on their controller.

Flick Stick can only turn the camera left and right. When Flick Stick is enabled, you can only look up and down using gyro controls.

In order to be able to look up and down at all times, we strongly recommend Flick Stick players set their Gyro Active Modes to ALWAYS or set Look Stick Effect to ENABLES. This lets you activate gyro aiming at any time by engaging the Flick Stick.
FortSetting.ReleaseToFire_Description: For sniper rifles. Instead of firing when you press the touch look & fire button, fire sniper rifles when you release the look & fire button. This means you can line up the shot with one finger and then fire with that same finger by releasing. Other fire buttons will still fire on press.
FortSetting.ContinuousFireGameplay_Description: For weapons that require you to release and press the trigger again for each shot, enabling this setting lets you just hold the trigger to fire repeatedly (at a reduced firing rate).
FortSetting.PredownloadCosmetics_Description:
Fortnite streams in cosmetic textures as they are encountered in-game. By turning this feature on, you will pre-download all of these textures up front instead of as they are encountered. This uses much more disk space but allows Fortnite to look more detailed without costing any bandwidth. This can improve Fortnite's visual quality if you frequently play using a cellular connection.

No: Fortnite will uninstall any previously downloaded cosmetic textures and will not pre-download them in the future.

Yes: Fortnite will download and install all high-quality textures up front.
FortSetting.ParsleyIdle_Description: Fortnite will go into Sleep Mode after you are inactive for the selected number of minutes. This setting applies to all devices where you are signed in.
FortSetting.FlickForwardDeadZone_Description: Forward angle where pointing the Flick Stick won't flick the camera (degrees).
FortSetting.FrameRateLimit_Description: Frame rate limit sets the highest frame rate that is allowed. Set this lower for a more consistent frame rate or higher for the best experience on faster machines. You may need to disable Vsync to reach high frame rates.
FortSetting.DisableSetting_GameCacheInGame: Game assets cannot be removed during gameplay.
FortSetting.EnableDLSSFrameGeneration_Description: Generates an additional frame for each rendered frame. Requires an NVIDIA 40 series RTX GPU
FortSetting.ShowChatPush_DescriptionMobile: Get notified when you have direct messages, group messages and group invites.
FortSetting.ShowInvitesPush_DescriptionMobile: Get notified when you have party invites or friend requests.
FortSetting.AutorunLockZonePreference_Description:
Gives the user the ability to keep running without requiring constant input.
Activate the Auto Run functionality by dragging your finger over the Auto Run Lock Zone while moving forward.

<strong>OFF</>: The Auto Run Lock Zone functionality is disabled.
<strong>CUSTOM</>: The Auto Run Lock Zone can be positioned manually through the HUD Layout Tool.
<strong>FIXED</>: The Auto Run Lock Zone is positioned above the move stick.
FortSetting.AutorunLockZonePreference_NoCustom_Descr:
Gives the user the ability to keep running without requiring constant input.
Activate the Auto Run functionality by dragging your finger over the Auto Run Lock Zone while moving forward.

<strong>OFF</>: The Auto Run Lock Zone functionality is disabled.
<strong>ON</>: The Auto Run Lock Zone is enabled and positioned above the move stick.
FortSetting.GlobalIllumination_Description:
Global Illumination controls the quality of ambient lighting.

The 'Lumen High' and 'Lumen Epic' options are much higher quality, but can reduce performance.

Off: Ambient lighting is unshadowed.

Ambient Occlusion: Lower quality ambient lighting with darkened corners.

Lumen High/Epic: High quality real-time global illumination.
FortSetting.TurnOnNotifications_Description:
Go to your device settings to turn notifications on.

When enabled, you will be notified when content downloads have completed, friend and party invites, new seasons, limited time events, announcements and promotions. Configure these at any time here in Fortnite Settings.
FortSetting.TurnOnNotificationsAndroid_Description:
Go to your device settings to turn notifications on.

When enabled, you will get notifications for content downloads, friend and party invites, new seasons, limited time events, announcements and promotions. Configure these at any time here in Fortnite Settings.

In order to download updates while using other apps notifications must be allowed.
FortSetting.DualShock4RequiredForGyro: Gyro Aiming on Windows requires a DUALSHOCK®4 controller or DualSense™ controller.
FortSetting.GyroAccelMaxThreshold_Description: Gyro aiming at this speed (in degrees per second) or faster will be multiplied by the Acceleration Multiplier if it's greater than 1, making your fast aiming even faster.
FortSetting.GyroAccelMinThreshold_Description: Gyro aiming at this speed (in degrees per second) or slower will be multiplied by the Acceleration Multiplier if it's less than 1, slowing your slow aiming even more.
FortSetting.GyroAccelMultiplier_Description:
Gyro aiming multiplier depending on how fast you're aiming. If this multiplier is greater than 1, it'll increase the sensitivity of your fast aiming (at or above the Max Threshold). If it's less than 1, it will decrease the sensitivity of your slow aiming (at or below the Min Threshold).

Aim speeds between the two thresholds reduce the effect of the multiplier. No fast multiplier is applied at or below the Min Threshold, and no slow multiplier is applied at or above the Max Threshold.
FortSetting.GyroControlsRequiredForFlickStick: Gyro aiming must be enabled to use Flick Stick.
FortSetting.GyroCheatSensitivity_Description: Gyro aiming sensitivity. Choose 1 for the camera to turn the same amount as the controller (unless using the Legacy acceleration mode).
FortSetting.GyroV2Sensitivity_Description: Gyro aiming sensitivity. Choose 1 for the camera to turn the same amount as the controller, 2 for the camera to turn twice as much, etc. The amount the camera turns is increased by your Acceleration settings and decreased as you zoom in with long-range weapons. You can toggle zoom scaling or change your sensitivity for different tools and aim modes by enabling Advanced Gyro Options below.
FortSetting.FPSMultiplierGyro_Description: Gyro sensitivity multiplier when playing in First Person Mode. This only affects weapons and game modes played in First Person even when not aiming down sights.
FortSetting.GyroV2ScopedSensitivity_Description: Gyro sensitivity multiplier while aiming with scoped weapons.
FortSetting.GyroV2TargetingSensitivity_Description: Gyro sensitivity multiplier while aiming your weapon.
FortSetting.GyroV2BuildingToolSensitivity_Descriptio: Gyro sensitivity multiplier while in build mode.
FortSetting.GyroV2EditingToolSensitivity_Description: Gyro sensitivity multiplier while in edit mode.
FortSetting.GyroV2HarvestingToolSensitivity_Descript: Gyro sensitivity multiplier while using the Pickaxe.
FortSetting.GyroCheatScopedSensitivity_Description: Gyro sensitivity while targeting with scoped weapons.
FortSetting.GyroCheatHarvestingToolSensitivity_Descr: Gyro sensitivity while using the Pickaxe.
FortSetting.GyroTighteningThreshold_Description: Helps keep your aim steady by reducing gyro aiming speed when your controller is turning very slowly. The threshold is how fast the controller needs to turn to disable this tightening effect (in degrees per second), and this effect can be disabled entirely by setting the threshold to 0. Gyro Deadzone is added to this threshold so that if both are enabled, Tightening provides a smooth transition out of the deadzone.
FortSetting.HideOtherPlayerNames_Name: Hide Other Display Names On Your Stream
FortSetting.AnonymousMode_Name: Hide Your Display Name From Stream Snipers
FortSetting.HideSocialName_Name: Hide Your Linked Social Account Names
FortSetting.HideSocialName_Description: Hides linked social media accounts from friends.
FortSetting.EnableNanite_Description: High-end geometry rendering through Nanite which improves geometry detail, but can lower framerate. Virtual Shadow Maps will be used when Nanite is enabled for higher quality shadowing.
FortSetting.MeshQuality_Description: Higher geometry settings gives objects a more detailed shape. Increasing this setting will make objects more detailed, but can reduce performance.
FortSetting.TextureQuality_Description_Default: Higher textures settings give flat objects a more detailed appearance. Increasing this setting will make objects more detailed, but can reduce performance.
FortSetting.TextureQuality_Description_NoHighMips:
Higher textures settings give flat objects a more detailed appearance. Increasing this setting will make objects more detailed, but can reduce performance.

High quality textures are not installed. They can be downloaded from the Epic Games Launcher in the Fortnite Installation Options.
FortSetting.ContinuousFire_Description: Holding the fire button will fire semi-automatic weapons repeatedly.
FortSetting.LeftTriggerDynamicReleaseThreshold_Descr: How far L2 needs to be released to stop an action bound to L2.  Lower values can make it easier to repeatedly do L2 actions over and over even if you don't always fully release L2,  but can also make it easier to trigger the L2 action accidentally.
FortSetting.RightTriggerDynamicReleaseThreshold_Desc: How far R2 needs to be released to stop an action bound to R2.  Lower values can make it easier to repeatedly do R2 actions over and over even if you don't always fully release R2,  but can also make it easier to trigger the R2 action accidentally.
FortSetting.TweakZoneScale_Description: How far Tweak Zone can move your crosshair.  Setting to 0 disables Tweak Zone.
FortSetting.LeftTriggerDynamicReleaseThreshold_Descr: How far the left trigger needs to be released to stop an action bound to the left trigger.  Lower values can make it easier to repeatedly do left trigger actions over and over even if you don't always fully release the left trigger,  but can also make it easier to trigger the left trigger action accidentally.
FortSetting.RightTriggerDynamicReleaseThreshold_Desc: How far the right trigger needs to be released to stop an action bound to the right trigger.  Lower values can make it easier to repeatedly do right trigger actions over and over even if you don't always fully release the right trigger,  but can also make it easier to trigger the right trigger action accidentally.
FortSetting.FlickTime_Description: How long a Flick Stick flick takes.
FortSetting.CameraResetTime_Description: How long it takes to reset the camera position when pressing the Reset Camera button.
FortSetting.CameraResetTime_Description_Switch: How long it takes to reset the camera position when pressing the Reset Camera button.
FortSetting.QuickWeaponDiamondDelay_Description:
How long the Quick Weapon button must be held for the Quick Weapon diamond to appear on the HUD. This delay only affects the visible HUD. It doesn't add any delay to using Quick Weapon itself. If you press and release the desired Quick Weapon combo in less than this time, the diamond won't show up on the HUD, but your equipped item will still change correctly.

Set this to its maximum value to never show the Quick Weapon diamond.
FortSetting.QuickWeaponDiamondDelay_Description_PS4:
How long the Quick Weapon button must be held for the Quick Weapon diamond to appear on the HUD. This delay only affects the visible HUD. It doesn't add any delay to using Quick Weapon itself. If you press and release the desired Quick Weapon combo in less than this time, the diamond won't show up on the HUD, but your equipped item will still change correctly.

Set this to its maximum value to never show the Quick Weapon diamond.
FortSetting.QuickWeaponDiamondDelay_Description_PS5:

FortSetting.SlideButtonHoldTime_Description: How long to hold the slide button to start sliding.
FortSetting.SlideButtonHoldTime_Description_Switch: How long to hold the slide button to start sliding.
FortSetting.MouseZoomScaling_Alt_Description: How mouse controls sensitivity scales down when the camera zooms in. LEGACY simply scales with the camera Field of View. STANDARD is similar but more accurately accounts for how a target's size and position on screen are affected by Field of View.
FortSetting.MouseZoomScaling_Description: How mouse sensitivity scales down when the camera zooms in. LEGACY simply scales with the camera Field of View. STANDARD is similar but more accurately accounts for how a target's size and position on screen are affected by Field of View.
FortSetting.AimAssistStrength_Description: How much aim assist to apply.  A setting of 0% will disable aim assist.  Lowering this setting is not recommended.
FortSetting.GyroSimpleSteadying_Description:
How much filtering to apply to gyro aiming when you're trying to keep your aim still or move it very little.

This slider controls the strength of Smoothing and Tightening filters, as well as whether to try to filter out controller vibration if the Vibration Feature is enabled in the Controller Options tab. More control over these filters is available under Advanced Gyro Options.
FortSetting.GyroSimpleSteadying_Description_PS4:
How much filtering to apply to gyro aiming when you're trying to keep your aim still or move it very little.

This slider controls the strength of Smoothing and Tightening filters, as well as whether to try to filter out controller vibration if the Vibration Feature is enabled in the Controller Options tab. More control over these filters is available under Advanced Gyro Options.

FortSetting.TouchLookAccelerationMultiplier_Descript: How much look acceleration to apply.
FortSetting.TweakZone_Description_Switch: How much of the active aiming area surrounding the Right Stick is used for tweaking your aim.  The Dead Zone is automatically included, so setting this to 0% will have the Tweak Zone exactly match the Dead Zone.  Increased values mean more overlap between tweaking your aim and turning the camera.
FortSetting.TweakZone_Description_PS4_PS5: How much of the active aiming area surrounding the right stick is used for tweaking your aim.  The Dead Zone is automatically included, so setting this to 0% will have the Tweak Zone exactly match the Dead Zone.  Increased values mean more overlap between tweaking your aim and turning the camera.
FortSetting.TweakZone_Description: How much of the active aiming area surrounding the stick is used for tweaking your aim.  The Dead Zone is automatically included, so setting this to 0% will have the Tweak Zone exactly match the Dead Zone.  Increased values mean more overlap between tweaking your aim and turning the camera.
FortSetting.LookHorizontalSpeedAds_Description: How quickly your view rotates left and right while aiming down sights.
FortSetting.LookHorizontalSpeed_Description: How quickly your view rotates left and right.
FortSetting.LookVerticalSpeedAds_Description: How quickly your view rotates up and down while aiming down sights.
FortSetting.LookVerticalSpeed_Description: How quickly your view rotates up and down.
FortSetting.LookSensitivityPresetAds_Description: How quickly your view rotates while aiming down sights (ADS).
FortSetting.GyroV2SpaceHandheld_Description_Switch:
How the motion inputs are converted to camera Yaw and Pitch rotations when playing in Handheld Mode.

LOCAL space means rotation is detected from the Nintendo Switch console's point of view, and will work the same whether you're lying down, sitting up, in zero gravity, etc. This means it's often the best option for handheld mode.

WORLD space detects motion relative to the direction of gravity, adapting to your position.

PLAYER space combines the precision of LOCAL space with the adaptiveness of WORLD space, making it often preferred for playing with a controller in TV mode or tabletop mode.
FortSetting.GyroV2SpaceHandheld_Description_Switch2:
How the motion inputs are converted to camera Yaw and Pitch rotations when playing in Handheld Mode.

LOCAL space means rotation is detected from the Nintendo Switch 2 console's point of view, and will work the same whether you're lying down, sitting up, in zero gravity, etc. This means it's often the best option for handheld mode.

WORLD space detects motion relative to the direction of gravity, adapting to your position.

PLAYER space combines the precision of LOCAL space with the adaptiveness of WORLD space, making it often preferred for playing with a controller in TV mode or tabletop mode.
FortSetting.GyroV2SpaceSwitch2_Description:
How the motion inputs are converted to camera Yaw and Pitch rotations when playing with a Nintendo Switch 2 Pro Controller or Joy‑Con 2 controllers detached from the console.

LOCAL space means rotation is detected from the Nintendo Switch 2 console's point of view, and will work the same whether you're lying down, sitting up, in zero gravity, etc. This means it's often the best option for handheld mode.

PLAYER space will detect motion relative to the direction of gravity, giving you the most freedom of movement and the most natural camera control when you're playing with a controller in TV mode or tabletop mode.
FortSetting.GyroV2SpaceSwitch_Description:
How the motion inputs are converted to camera Yaw and Pitch rotations when playing with a Nintendo Switch Pro Controller or Joy‑Con controllers detached from the console.

LOCAL space means rotation is detected from the Nintendo Switch console's point of view, and will work the same whether you're lying down, sitting up, in zero gravity, etc. This means it's often the best option for handheld mode.

WORLD space detects motion relative to the direction of gravity, adapting to your position.

PLAYER space combines the precision of LOCAL space with the adaptiveness of WORLD space, making it often preferred for playing with a controller in TV mode or tabletop mode.
FortSetting.GyroV2Space_Description:
How the motion inputs are converted to camera Yaw and Pitch rotations.

LOCAL space means rotation is detected from the device's point of view, and will work the same whether you're lying down, sitting up, in zero gravity, etc. This means it's often the best option for handheld gaming.

WORLD space detects motion relative to the direction of gravity, adapting to your position.

PLAYER space combines the precision of LOCAL space with the adaptiveness of WORLD space, making it often preferred for playing with a controller in front of a fixed screen.
FortSetting.FilterControllerVibrationFromGyro_Descri: If controller's vibration feature is enabled, add a narrow filter to reduce camera vibration. This can add a tiny bit of input latency to gyro aiming. This filter is automatically disabled if the Vibration Feature is disabled in the Controller Options tab.
FortSetting.CrossplayPreference_Description: If enabled you can participate in parties and game modes with other platforms. If disabled, some game modes will be unavailable, and others may have Limited Pools with longer queue times, lower match populations, or higher latency.
FortSetting.HoldToSwapPickup_Description: If enabled, current weapon can be swapped with pickup on the ground by holding use.
FortSetting.SwapFireInputsDualWieldShotgunSMG_Descri: If enabled, fire buttons on mouse will be swapped for Pump & Dump weapons.
FortSetting.GyroButtonHoldTime_Description: If the Gyro Modifier button is shared with a short-press action, this is how long the button needs to be held to skip the short-press action.
FortSetting.GyroCalibrationContext_Description2:
If the motion sensor's zero point isn't calibrated correctly, it can give the wrong values and aim incorrectly. The system automatically detects when the controller is still to recalibrate. This can sometimes have false positives during slow and steady movements, so this setting determines when calibration can happen:

ANY TIME means motion sensor recalibration may occur at any time, even while aiming if your aim is very steady.

MENUS ONLY means motion sensor recalibration won't happen during regular gameplay. To calibrate during gameplay, open the sidebar or menu and then put the controller down for about 3 seconds.
FortSetting.GyroCalibrationContext_Description3:
If the motion sensor's zero point isn't calibrated correctly, it can give the wrong values and aim incorrectly. The system automatically detects when the controller is still to recalibrate. This can sometimes have false positives during slow and steady movements, so this setting determines when calibration can happen:

ANY TIME means motion sensor recalibration may occur at any time, even while aiming if your aim is very steady.

MENUS ONLY means motion sensor recalibration won't happen during regular gameplay. To calibrate during gameplay, open the sidebar or menu and then put the controller down for about 3 seconds.

LOCKED locks in the current motion sensor calibration, making sure it won't change unless the game is stopped or the controller is disconnected. To reset your calibration, change this setting to ANY TIME or MENUS ONLY (no need to press APPLY), put the controller down for about 3 seconds, and then change this setting back to LOCKED to lock it in.

FortSetting.LicensedAudioTreatment_Description:
If you are a content creator, you can select this option to avoid playing licensed music from audio sources and also avoid viewing copyrighted videos from any video player. This setting does not apply to event playlists that feature content that is licensed like concerts.

<strong>Play</>: The audio is played normally (Default).

<strong>Mute</>: All sources containing licensed audio will be muted.

FortSetting.IgnoreGamepadInput_Description: Ignore all controller input.  If you are using a input remapping tool, remapping controller input to keyboard keys, and don't want the game to also receive the controller input.
FortSetting.GyroDeadZone_Description: Ignore gyro input below this threshold (degrees per second). This can make it impossible to track a distant or slow-moving target depending on your sensitivity settings, so it's usually preferable to try stabilize your aim using the Smoothing or Tightening options above before resorting to this.
FortSetting.WindowMode_NonexclusiveDescription: In Windowed mode you can drag the edges of the window to set the size. In Windowed Fullscreen mode the window will cover the entire display.
FortSetting.WindowMode_Description: In Windowed mode you can interact with other windows more easily, and drag the edges of the window to set the size. In Windowed Fullscreen mode you can easily switch between applications. In Fullscreen mode you cannot interact with other windows as easily, but the game may run slightly faster.

FortSetting.FlickDeadZone_Description_Switch: It's better for the flick to only be triggered towards the outer edge of the Stick's range, because that will give a more accurate measure of your intended angle. So this DeadZone is how far between the Right Stick DeadZone below and the outer edge that Flick Stick should actually be triggered.

FortSetting.HideOtherPlayerNames_Description:
NOTE: This feature is only intended to help streamers mitigate stream sniping.

Hide other players' display names in the elimination feed, spectator view, and select other places in your gameplay experience.

<strong>You will always see the display name of players who are in your party, squad, or communicate with you using voice or text chat.</>

Some experiences will not allow you to hide other player names, including Tournament modes and Replays of matches where it was enabled.

<strong>ON</>: Other player display names will be replaced with a variant of "Player" only where applicable (see above).

<strong>OFF</>: Other player display names will appear as normal.
FortSetting.AnonymousMode_Description:
NOTE: This feature is only intended to help streamers mitigate stream sniping.

Hide your display name in the elimination feed, spectator view, and select other places that could reveal to another player that you're in their match.

<strong>Players who are in your party, squad, and players with whom you communicate using voice or text chat will always be able to see your display name.</>

Some experiences will not allow you to hide your display name, including Tournament modes and Replays of matches where it was enabled.

<strong>ON</>: Your display name will be replaced with a variant of "Anonymous" only where applicable (see above).

<strong>OFF</>: Your display name will appear as normal.
FortSetting.DLSS_Description: NVIDIA DLSS (Deep Learning Super Sampling). NVIDIA DLSS uses deep learning to render highly detailed images using fewer pixels, boosting your frame rates so you can maximize your graphics settings and output resolution. DLSS requires an NVIDIA RTX graphics card.

Reflection quality determines the resolution and accuracy of reflections.

The 'Lumen High' and 'Lumen Epic' options are much higher quality, but can reduce performance.

Off: Reflections are turned off.

Screen Space: Calculates reflections based only on what is currently shown on the screen.

Lumen High/Epic: High quality real-time ray traced reflections.

FortSetting.RenderingModeNoDX11_Description:
Selects which low-level rendering mode to use.

DirectX 12 is required to enable high-end rendering features for enhanced visual fidelity such as Nanite, Lumen, and Hardware Ray-Tracing.

Performance is a mode that offers significant performance gains by disabling most rendering features to reduce memory usage and lighten the load on CPU and GPU. It also uses DirectX 12.

Legacy Performance (Deprecated) is like Performance mode, but uses DirectX 11.
FortSetting.RenderingMode_TwoModeDescription:
Selects which low-level rendering mode to use.

DirectX 12 is required to enable high-end rendering features for enhanced visual fidelity such as Nanite, Lumen, and Hardware Ray-Tracing.

Performance is a mode that offers significant performance gains by disabling most rendering features to reduce memory usage and lighten the load on CPU and GPU. It uses DirectX 12 when possible and DirectX 11 otherwise.

FortSetting.GyroV2Button_Description:
Set a button to pause gyro aiming or change gyro behavior while this button is pressed. This lets you reset your controller position without unintentionally turning the camera. Gyro aiming will be paused while this button is held, but you can still use the button's regular action with a short press.

You can also set Gyro Modifier buttons in the controller customization tab.

You can set this button to do something other than pause gyro, such as enable or invert it, under Advanced Gyro Options.

FortSetting.TimeLimitsActionDescription: Set limits for the total time your child spends in Fortnite, Creative, and Unreal Editor for Fortnite (UEFN).

FortSetting.TouchDragSensitivity_Description: Set the sensitivity of touch and drag. With higher settings the camera will move faster when dragging to look around.
