FortSetting.ShowTextChat_Description:
Sets scope of other players you wish to text chat with. You can only send Direct Messages to and receive Direct Messages from players on your friends list. 

<strong>Everybody</>: You can send text chat messages to and receive text chat messages from everyone in Game and Party Channels.

<strong>Friends and Teammates</>: You can send text chat messages to and receive text chat messages from friends and teammates in Game and Party Channels. Team size will vary depending on the experience, ranging from a couple of players to all participants in a game session. You can view the list of team members in the Sidebar.

<strong>Friends Only</>: You can send text chat messages to and receive text chat messages from your friends in Game and Party Channels. Players who are not friends with you won't be able to send you text chat messages, and they won't receive your messages.

<strong>Off</>: Text chat and text reporting are off.

<strong>About Text Reporting</>
When text reporting is on, it enables the contents of your text chat channels to be sent to Epic Games only if a participant reports a violation. Text reporting is usually on, except in Direct Messages and parties formed among friends where all players prefer text reporting off. You can see if text reporting is currently on or off in the text input field before typing a message. Text reporting is always on for players under 18.{0}

See the text reporting setting for more details and options.
FortSetting.VoiceChat_ExpandedSettings_Description:
Sets scope of other players you wish to voice chat with.

<strong>Everybody</>: You can hear everyone and can also talk to them by using a microphone.

<strong>Friends and Teammates</>: You can hear friends and teammates and can also talk to them by using a microphone.

<strong>Friends Only</>: You can hear friends and can also talk to them by using a microphone.

<strong>Nobody</>: Epic voice chat is disabled.
FortSetting.VoiceChat_VRR_ExpandedSettings_Descripti:
Sets scope of other players you wish to voice chat with. Turning on chat also turns on voice reporting.

<strong>Everybody</>: You can hear everyone and can also talk to them by using a microphone.

<strong>Friends and Teammates</>: You can hear friends and teammates and can also talk to them by using a microphone. Team size will vary depending on the experience, ranging from a couple of players to all participants in a game session. You can view the list of team members in the Sidebar.

<strong>Friends Only</>: You can hear friends and can also talk to them by using a microphone.

<strong>Off</>: Voice chat and voice reporting is off.

<strong>About voice reporting</>
When voice chat is on, voice reporting stores voice chat audio on each participant's device on a rolling five-minute basis, which will be sent to Epic Games only if a participant reports a violation. Voice reporting is usually on, except in parties formed among friends where all players prefer voice reporting off. You can see if voice reporting is currently on or off in the Sidebar. Voice reporting is always on for players under 18.

See the voice reporting setting for more details and options.
FortSetting.ToggleSprint_Description: Sets sprint to be an on/off toggle or a hold to sprint. When on, sprint is set to an on/off toggle with one press.
FortSetting.RudderDeadZone_Description: Sets the Dead Zone of the foot controller.
FortSetting.RudderMaxThrottle_Description: Sets the max throttle point of the foot controller.
FortSetting.GamepadBuildingMultiplier_Description: Sets the modifier for controller sensitivity when in build mode. 1x will have no change when in build mode. Lower settings will have more slow down when in build mode, and higher settings will increase sensitivity when in build mode.
FortSetting.GamepadEditModeMultiplier_Description: Sets the modifier for controller sensitivity when in edit mode. 1x will have no change when in edit mode. Lower settings will have more slow down when in edit mode, and higher settings will increase sensitivity when in edit mode.
FortSetting.FPSMultiplierController_Description: Sets the modifier for look sensitivity when playing in First Person Mode. This only affects weapons and game modes played in First Person even when not aiming down sights. 100% will have no slow down or increase while in First Person.
FortSetting.MouseBuildingMultiplier_AltMouse_Descrip: Sets the modifier for mouse controls sensitivity when building. 100% will have no slow down or increase when building.
FortSetting.MouseEditingMultiplier_AltMouse_Descript: Sets the modifier for mouse controls sensitivity when editing. 100% will have no slow down or increase when editing.
FortSetting.FPSMultiplierMouse_Alt_Description: Sets the modifier for mouse controls sensitivity when playing in First Person Mode. This only affects weapons and game modes played in First Person even when not targeting your weapon. 100% will have no slow down or increase while in First Person.
FortSetting.MouseBuildingMultiplier_Description: Sets the modifier for mouse sensitivity when building. 100% will have no slow down or increase when building.
FortSetting.MouseEditingMultiplier_Description: Sets the modifier for mouse sensitivity when editing. 100% will have no slow down or increase when editing.
FortSetting.FPSMultiplierMouse_Description: Sets the modifier for mouse sensitivity when playing in First Person Mode. This only affects weapons and game modes played in First Person even when not targeting your weapon. 100% will have no slow down or increase while in First Person.
FortSetting.GamepadTargetingMultiplier_Description: Sets the modifier for reducing controller sensitivity when targeting. 100% will have no slow down when targeting. Lower settings will have more slow down when targeting.
FortSetting.GamepadScopedMultiplier_Description: Sets the modifier for reducing controller sensitivity when using a scope. 100% will have no slow down when using a scope. Lower settings will have more slow down when scoped.
FortSetting.MouseTargetingMultiplier_Alt_Description: Sets the modifier for reducing mouse controls sensitivity when targeting. 100% will have no slow down when targeting. Lower settings will have more slow down when targeting.
FortSetting.MouseScopedMultiplier_Alt_Description: Sets the modifier for reducing mouse controls sensitivity when using a scope. 100% will have no slow down when using a scope. Lower settings will have more slow down when scoped.
FortSetting.MouseTargetingMultiplier_Description: Sets the modifier for reducing mouse sensitivity when targeting. 100% will have no slow down when targeting. Lower settings will have more slow down when targeting.
FortSetting.MouseScopedMultiplier_Description: Sets the modifier for reducing mouse sensitivity when using a scope. 100% will have no slow down when using a scope. Lower settings will have more slow down when scoped.
FortSetting.TouchDragScopedSensitivity_Description: Sets the modifier for reducing touch and drag sensitivity when using a scope. 100% will have no slow down when using a scope. Lower settings will have more slow down when scoped.
FortSetting.TouchBuildingMultiplier_Description: Sets the modifier for touch sensitivity when in build mode. 100% will have no change when in build mode. Lower settings will have more slow down when in build mode, and higher settings will increase sensitivity when in build mode.
FortSetting.TouchEditModeMultiplier_Description: Sets the modifier for touch sensitivity when in edit mode. 100% will have no change when in edit mode. Lower settings will have more slow down when in edit mode, and higher settings will increase sensitivity when in edit mode.
FortSetting.ControllerLookSensitivityYaw_Description: Sets the sensitivity of the Right Stick's horizontal (x) axis. With higher settings the camera will move faster when looking left and right with the Right Stick.
FortSetting.ControllerLookSensitivityPitch_Descripti: Sets the sensitivity of the Right Stick's vertical (y) axis. With higher settings the camera will move faster when looking up and down with the Right Stick.
FortSetting.MouseSensitivityYaw_Alt_Description: Sets the sensitivity of the mouse controls' horizontal (x) axis. With higher settings the camera will move faster when looking left and right with the Joy‑Con 2 controller(s).
FortSetting.MouseSensitivityPitch_Alt_Description: Sets the sensitivity of the mouse controls' vertical (y) axis. With higher settings the camera will move faster when looking up and down with the Joy‑Con 2 controller(s).
FortSetting.MouseSensitivityYaw_Description: Sets the sensitivity of the mouse's horizontal (x) axis. With higher settings the camera will move faster when looking left and right with the mouse.
FortSetting.MouseSensitivityPitch_Description: Sets the sensitivity of the mouse's vertical (y) axis. With higher settings the camera will move faster when looking up and down with the mouse.
FortSetting.ControllerLookSensitivityYaw_Description: Sets the sensitivity of the right stick's horizontal (x) axis. With higher settings the camera will move faster when looking left and right with the right stick.
Sets the sensitivity of the right stick's horizontal (x) axis. With higher settings the camera will move faster when looking left and right with the right stick.
FortSetting.ControllerLookSensitivityPitch_Descripti: Sets the sensitivity of the right stick's vertical (y) axis. With higher settings the camera will move faster when looking up and down with the right stick.
Sets the sensitivity of the right stick's vertical (y) axis. With higher settings the camera will move faster when looking up and down with the right stick.
FortSetting.HUDScale_DescriptionMobile: Sets the size of heads up display (HUD) elements when using a controller or a Keyboard and mouse. 100% will show the default HUD size. Lower the settings will make HUD elements smaller.
FortSetting.HUDScale_Description: Sets the size of heads up display (HUD) elements. 100% will show the default HUD size. Lower settings will make HUD elements smaller.
FortSetting.SoundQuality_Description: Sets the sound quality. 'High' quality sounds better, but may decrease performance.
FortSetting.EForceMaxTouchpadSensitivity_Description: Sets the touchpad sensitivity so that keyboard usage will not suppress touchpad mouse events.  This greatly improves the trackpad as a game control, however if a mouse is being used accidental touchpad touches may register.
FortSetting.FPSMultiplierTouch_Description: Sets touch sensitivity when playing game modes in First Person, even when not Aiming Down Sights. 100% means no change.
FortSetting.TouchDragTargetingSensitivity_Descriptio: Sets touch sensitivity when you Aim Down Sights. 100% means no slowdown. Lower values make aiming more precise.
FortSetting.Setting Enabled: Setting Disabled when Performance is not selected.
FortSetting.Setting Disabled: Setting Disabled when Performance is selected.
FortSetting.GyroV2Enabled_SettingWarning: Setting is not supported while Gyro Aiming is enabled.
FortSetting.ResolutionScale_Warning: Setting the 3D resolution to a higher value than the device's capacity may impact performance.
FortSetting.ResolutionScale_LowValue_Warning: Setting the 3D resolution to values below 10% may negatively impact game playability.
FortSetting.Shadows_Description: Shadow quality determines the resolution and view distance of dynamic shadows. Shadows improve visual quality and give better depth perception, but can reduce performance. Virtual Shadow Maps will be used when Nanite is enabled for even higher quality.
FortSetting.SocialPrivacy_ShareActivity_Description:
Share Activity allows you to share your play history and online presence with the Fortnite community.

ON: When turned on, your online presence will be publicly visible and the games that you are playing will be posted to your Creator page under the Shared Play History section.

OFF: When turned off, your online presence will not be publicly visible. Also, gameplay that occurs when this option is off will not be posted publicly to your Creator Page.
FortSetting.GyroActiveCheatMode_Description: Should gyro always be active, or only when aiming/firing? If always active, it's good to set something else to disable gyro.
FortSetting.UseBothQuickbarsLayout_Description: Show both Quickbars at the same time.  When off, only the active quickbar will be displayed (combat or building)
FortSetting.ContextTutorial_Description: Show helpful tips for new players.
FortSetting.MapPickupDotRarity_Description: Show pickup indicators for ingredients, ammo, and building materials on the map at or above the specified rarity
FortSetting.ShowSpeechToText_Description: Show the button in chat so you can use your voice to type text chat messages. Available in English.
FortSetting.STW_ShowGrass_Description: Shows grass in the game, which can reduce performance on slower machines. Disable to hide grass and improve performance.
FortSetting.FullRangeTest_Description: Shows the full grayscale range calibration UI.
FortSetting.SimpleBuildSTW_Name: Simple Build in Save The World
FortSetting.QuickEditSTW_Name: Simple Edit in Save The World
FortSetting.GyroSmoothingThreshold_Description: Smooth gyro input that's below this threshold (degrees per second). Set to 0 to disable smoothing entirely. Gyro Deadzone is added to this threshold so that the Deadzone setting never cancels out your Smoothing Threshold.
FortSetting.QualcommGSR_Description: Snapdragon GSR is a single pass spatial aware super resolution technique developed by the Qualcomm Snapdragon Studios to achieve optimal super scaling quality at the best performance and power savings.
FortSetting.Snapdragon GSR is Disabled: Snapdragon GSR is disabled in the Anti Aliasing & Super Resolution setting.
FortSetting.UnsupportedD3D12: Software or hardware requirements are not met. DirectX 12 requires a newer version of Windows 10 (newer than version 1909.1350) or Windows 11 with support for DirectX 12 Agility SDK, a dedicated graphics card with advanced shading features, and the latest graphics drivers.
FortSetting.UserInterfaceParallax_Description: Some menus like the Item Shop may have parallax effects. Disable this setting for those effects to remain static.
FortSetting.MotionBlur_Description: Some people are very sensitive to motion blur. Disable this effect to prevent nausea or keep it enabled to get more fluent animations.
FortSetting.FlickStickDisablesStandardStickSettings: Standard stick settings are disabled because Flick Stick is enabled in the Touch & Motion tab.
FortSetting.ShowMarketingInvitesPush_DescriptionMobi: Stay up to date with what is happening in Fortnite! Get notified about new seasons, limited time events, announcements and promotions.
FortSetting.StopRenderingInBackground_Description: Stop rendering when game window is not focused when possible. Frees up GPU resources for other applications.
FortSetting.SwapFireInputsDualWieldShotgunSMG_Name: Swap Fire Buttons for Pump & Dump
FortSetting.HybridMouseSwapAxes_Description: Swap movement control from Left Stick to Right Stick.
FortSetting.TapToInteractMobile_Name: Tap to Search / Interact With Controller
FortSetting.TSR is Disabled: Temporal Super Resolution is disabled in the Anti Aliasing & Super Resolution setting.
FortSetting.TSR is Enabled: Temporal Super Resolution is enabled in the Anti Aliasing & Super Resolution setting. Use the Temporal Super Resolution setting to control the 3D resolution rendered every frame.
FortSetting.LumenRequiresD3D12: The 'Rendering Mode' setting must be set to 'DirectX 12' in order to enable Lumen.
FortSetting.NaniteRequiresD3D12: The 'Rendering Mode' setting must be set to 'DirectX 12' in order to enable Nanite.
FortSetting.FilterMatureLanguage_Warning: The Mature Language filter is always on for players under 13.
FortSetting.ButtonHoldTime_Description: The amount of time (in seconds) that holding a button will cause it to be selected when using a touchscreen. Only applies to experiences made in UEFN
FortSetting.KeyboardMovementBackwardDiagonal_Descrip: The angle to run when entering a backward diagonal with keyboard.
FortSetting.KeyboardMovementForwardDiagonal_Descript: The angle to run when entering a forward diagonal with keyboard.
FortSetting.KeyboardMovementStrafeDiagonal_Descripti: The angle to run when strafing with keyboard.
FortSetting.Display_Description: The display on which the window should be displayed.
FortSetting.EditButtonHoldTime_Description: The duration to hold the button for entering Edit Mode.
FortSetting.EditButtonHoldTime_Description_Switch: The duration to hold the button for entering Edit Mode.
FortSetting.SimpleEditButtonHoldTime_Description: The duration to hold the button to edit and reset while using Simple Edit.
FortSetting.SimpleEditButtonHoldTime_Description_Swi: The duration to hold the button to edit and reset while using Simple Edit.
FortSetting.FOVMaximum_Description: The maximum allowed Field of View (FOV) during gameplay.
FortSetting.FOVMinimum_Description: The minimum allowed Field of View (FOV) during gameplay.
FortSetting.TriggerHapticsStartPosition_Description: The start position of the Adaptive Trigger effects.
FortSetting.TriggerHapticsStrength_Description: The strength of the Adaptive Trigger effect.
FortSetting.LookBoostAccelerationTimeAds_Description: The time it takes to reach the boost speed when the Right Stick is fully deflected while aiming down sights.  A setting of 0.0 seconds will cause the boost to kick in instantly.
The time it takes to reach the boost speed when the Right Stick is fully deflected while aiming down sights.  A setting of 0.0 seconds will cause the boost to kick in instantly.
FortSetting.LookBoostAccelerationTime_Description_Sw: The time it takes to reach the boost speed when the Right Stick is fully deflected.  A setting of 0.0 seconds will cause the boost to kick in instantly.

FortSetting.LookBoostAccelerationTime_Description_PS: The time it takes to reach the boost speed when the right stick is fully deflected.  A setting of 0.0 seconds will cause the boost to kick in instantly.
The time it takes to reach the boost speed when the right stick is fully deflected.  A setting of 0.0 seconds will cause the boost to kick in instantly.
FortSetting.LookBoostAccelerationTime_Description_XB: The time it takes to reach the boost speed when the right stick is fully deflected.  A setting of 0.0 seconds will cause the boost to kick in instantly.
FortSetting.LookBoostAccelerationTime_Description_XS: The time it takes to reach the boost speed when the right stick is fully deflected.  A setting of 0.0 seconds will cause the boost to kick in instantly.
FortSetting.LookEaseTime_Description_Switch: The time it takes to reach the expected look rotation speed after applying initial input to the Right Stick.  This allows for small view adjustments by tapping the Stick.  Set to 0.0 seconds to disable the dampening.
FortSetting.LookEaseTime_Description_Switch2: The time it takes to reach the expected look rotation speed after applying initial input to the Right Stick.  This allows for small view adjustments by tapping the Stick.  Set to 0.0 seconds to disable the dampening.
FortSetting.LookEaseTime_Description: The time it takes to reach the expected look rotation speed after applying initial input to the right stick.  This allows for small view adjustments by tapping the stick.  Set to 0.0 seconds to disable the dampening.
FortSetting.LookEaseTime_Description_PS4: The time it takes to reach the expected look rotation speed after applying initial input to the right stick.  This allows for small view adjustments by tapping the stick.  Set to 0.0 seconds to disable the dampening.
FortSetting.LookEaseTime_Description_PS5: The time it takes to reach the expected look rotation speed after applying initial input to the right stick.  This allows for small view adjustments by tapping the stick.  Set to 0.0 seconds to disable the dampening.
FortSetting.LookEaseTime_Description_XB1: The time it takes to reach the expected look rotation speed after applying initial input to the right stick.  This allows for small view adjustments by tapping the stick.  Set to 0.0 seconds to disable the dampening.
FortSetting.LookEaseTime_Description_XSX: The time it takes to reach the expected look rotation speed after applying initial input to the right stick.  This allows for small view adjustments by tapping the stick.  Set to 0.0 seconds to disable the dampening.
FortSetting.AdvancedGyroWarning: These Advanced Gyro Options can be confusing for beginners at gyro aiming, so they're usually best left disabled when starting out.
FortSetting.ReportPerfStats_Description: This feature enables the upload of detailed performance stats for the next several matches, or until you exit the game.
FortSetting.LowInputLatencyMode_Description: This feature greatly <strong>reduces input lag</>, but may have a minor impact on framerate in some situations.
FortSetting.GyroV2TouchMode_Description: This lets you activate or deactivate gyro aiming by touching the look region of your screen, regardless of your Active Mode settings.
FortSetting.GyroV2StickMode_Description_Switch:
This lets you force gyro aiming off or on by using the Right Stick on your controller, regardless of your Active Mode settings.

Setting to DISABLES can make it easier to adjust your camera with the Right Stick without being affected by controller movements. Setting to ENABLES can be useful with Flick Stick, giving you a way to activate gyro controls at any time so you can still look up and down.
FortSetting.GyroV2StickMode_Description_Switch2:

Setting to DISABLES can make it easier to adjust your camera with the Right Stick without being affected by controller movements. Setting to ENABLES can be useful with Flick Stick, giving you a way to activate gyro controls at any time so you can still look up and down.

FortSetting.AllowPersonalizationExperience_Descripti:
This setting allows the messages you receive about Fortnite experiences to be personalized. Examples of these types of messages would be announcements or recommendations for Fortnite in-game events we think you might like.

<strong>ON</>: Messages about Fortnite experiences can be personalized

<strong>OFF</>: Messages about Fortnite experiences will not be personalized
FortSetting.AllowPersonalizationPurchase_Description:
This setting allows you to see Fortnite Item Shop offers we think you might like. You’ll also receive personalized emails or in-game messages about offers and new items coming to the shop.

<strong>ON</>: You will receive personalized offers or messages in Fortnite.

<strong>OFF</>: You will still see offers and receive messages, but they will not be personalized.
FortSetting.TapQuickEditWarning: This setting does not affect controllers when using the default binding to confirm edits.
FortSetting.AllowDiscoveryPersonalization_Descriptio:
This setting enables you to receive Island and Creator Recommendations based upon information we have about you and your play history.

<strong>ON</>: You will receive additional Island and Creator recommendations based upon information we have about you and your play history.

<strong>OFF</>: You will not receive additional Island and Creator recommendations based upon information we have about you and your play history. You can still customize your recommendations using favorited Collections.
FortSetting.SocialStatus_PartyJoinability_Descriptio:
This setting is for how players join your party.

<strong>Invite Only</>: You or someone in your party have to invite players or accept their requests to join in order for them to join the party.

<strong>Friends</>: Players on your friends list and friends of party members will be able to join your party at any time.

NOTE: A party can always be joined via invite.
FortSetting.MatureLanguageFilter_Description:
This setting lets you filter mature language in text chat messages in Party Channels and Direct Messages. The Mature Language filter will always be on in Game Channels and for players under 13.

When this setting is active, mature language that you send or receive will be replaced with heart symbols.


<strong>ON</>: Mature language will be filtered and replaced with heart symbols.
<strong>OFF</>: Mature language can appear in text chat.
FortSetting.FilterMatureLanguage_Desc:
This setting lets you filter mature language in text chat messages in Party Channels and Direct Messages. The Mature Language filter will always be on in Game Channels.

When this setting is active, mature language that you send or receive will be replaced with heart symbols.


<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">ON</>: Mature language will be filtered and replaced with heart symbols.
<text color="00FFD6FF" fontface="black" case="upper" size="20">OFF</>: Mature language can appear in text chat.
FortSetting.PIIFilter_Description:
This setting lets you filter personal information, like email addresses and credit card numbers, in text chat messages in Party Channels and Direct Messages. When this setting is active, certain types of personal information that you send or receive will be replaced with heart symbols.

The Personal Information filter will always be on in Game Channels and for players under the age of 13. 

<strong>ON</>: Personal information will be filtered and replaced with heart symbols.
<strong>OFF</>: Personal information can appear in text chat.
FortSetting.PreferredFilledMatchAudioChannel_Descrip:
This setting only applies when you choose to play a mode while already in a party and have Fill turned ON.

Fill is when your empty slots are filled with random players. Note: this setting does not apply when playing a game mode by yourself.

<strong>GAME:</>

By selecting this Game setting your voice chat will leave the Party Channel and automatically be switched into the Game Channel and you will be able to chat with the random players on your team.

<strong>PARTY:</>

By selecting this Party setting you will stay in the Party Voice Channel and will have to manually switch to the Game Channel in order to chat with the random players on your team.
FortSetting.FlickSmoothingThreshold_Description: Threshold above which no smoothing will be applied to rotations (rotations per second).
FortSetting.TimeLimitsActionWarning: Time Limits set for your device or console operate independently of your Fortnite Time Limits, and may further limit allowed time on that device.
FortSetting.QuickWeaponOnPress_Description:
To select your primary weapon, press and release the Quick Weapon button without pressing one of the other weapon buttons. By default, the primary weapon is only selected after releasing the Quick Weapon button, and not the instant it is pressed. This is to help players learn to press button combos to select other weapons. But for those who want to switch to their primary weapon as quickly as possible, enable this setting. This will equip your primary weapon the instant Quick Weapon is pressed.

This has no effect on selecting slots 2-5, which already happen the instant the corresponding button is pressed.

FortSetting.ReticleShowsSpread_Description: Toggle whether the reticle will expand to show your weapon's spread (the range of directions in which shots might go) in First Person game modes.
FortSetting.ReticleShowsSpreadThirdPerson_Descriptio: Toggle whether the reticle will expand to show your weapon's spread (the range of directions in which shots might go) in Third Person game modes.
FortSetting.GyroV2AimAssist_Description: Trigger aim assist when gyro aiming.
FortSetting.STW_AutoEquipBetterItems_Description: Turn auto equip better items on/off. When on, better items will automatically be equipped when they are received.
FortSetting.AutoInteractContainers_Description: Turns Auto Open Containers on/off. When on, Chests and other containers will automatically open when you are near them.
FortSetting.AutoInteractRevives_Description: Turns Auto Revive Teammate on/off. When on, you will automatically start to revive a DBNO teammate when you stand near them.
FortSetting.AutoInteractZiplines_Description: Turns Auto Use Ziplines on/off. When on, moving over a Zipline or Ascender will make you automatically use them.
FortSetting.ShadowPlayHighlights_Description: Turns NVIDIA highlights on/off. When on, NVIDIA highlights are automatically recorded for certain game events, like winning, getting eliminations, or being eliminated. This feature requires specific NVIDIA hardware.
FortSetting.TouchButtonPulseAnimation_Description: Turns Pulse Animation on/off. When on, buttons display a Pulse Animation in certain situations.
FortSetting.AimAssist_Description: Turns aim assist on/off. When on, this helps attract your aim towards enemy players when using a controller.
FortSetting.TouchAimAssist_Description: Turns aim assist on/off. When on, this helps attract your aim towards enemy players.
FortSetting.InvertPitchForAircraftPrimary_Descriptio: Turns aircraft controller steering inversion on/off. When on, forward movement will pitch the aircraft down.
FortSetting.InvertPitchForAircraftSecondary_Descript: Turns aircraft mouse steering inversion on/off. When on, moving the mouse forward will pitch the aircraft down.
FortSetting.InvertPitchForAircraftTouch_Description: Turns aircraft touch steering inversion on/off. When on, moving touch forward will pitch the aircraft down.
FortSetting.AutoSortConsumablesToHigherSlots_Descrip: Turns auto sort consumables to higher numbered slots on/off. When on, consumable items such as health and shields will default to the highest empty quickbar slot when picked up.
FortSetting.AutoSortConsumablesToRight_Description: Turns auto sort consumables to right on/off. When on, consumable items such as health and shields will default to the rightmost empty quickbar slot when picked up.
FortSetting.GamepadAutoRun_Description_PS4: Turns controller auto-run on/off. When on, pressing the L3 Button twice will make you automatically run forward without needing to hold the left stick forward.
FortSetting.GamepadAutoRun_Description_PS5: Turns controller auto-run on/off. When on, pressing the L3 Button twice will make you automatically run forward without needing to hold the left stick forward.
FortSetting.GamepadAutoRun_Description_Switch: Turns controller auto-run on/off. When on, pressing the Left Stick twice will make you automatically run forward without needing to hold the Left Stick forward.
FortSetting.GamepadAutoRun_Description_Switch2: Turns controller auto-run on/off. When on, pressing the Left Stick twice will make you automatically run forward without needing to hold the Left Stick forward.
FortSetting.GamepadAutoRun_Description: Turns controller auto-run on/off. When on, pressing the left stick twice will make you automatically run forward without needing to hold the left stick forward.
FortSetting.GamepadAutoRun_Description_XB1: Turns controller auto-run on/off. When on, pressing the left stick twice will make you automatically run forward without needing to hold the left stick forward.
FortSetting.GamepadAutoRun_Description_XSX: Turns controller auto-run on/off. When on, pressing the left stick twice will make you automatically run forward without needing to hold the left stick forward.
FortSetting.InvertPitch_Controller_Description: Turns controller look inversion on/off.  When on, your character will look down when the Right Stick is moved up.
FortSetting.InvertPitch_Controller_Description_Switc: Turns controller look inversion on/off.  When on, your character will look down when the Right Stick is moved up.
Turns controller look inversion on/off.  When on, your character will look down when the Right Stick is moved up.
FortSetting.InvertPitch_Controller_Description_XB1: Turns controller look inversion on/off.  When on, your character will look down when the Right Stick is moved up.
FortSetting.InvertPitch_Controller_Description_XSX: Turns controller look inversion on/off.  When on, your character will look down when the Right Stick is moved up.
FortSetting.InvertPitch_Controller_Description_PS4: Turns controller look inversion on/off.  When on, your character will look down when the right stick is moved up.
FortSetting.InvertPitch_Controller_Description_PS5: Turns controller look inversion on/off.  When on, your character will look down when the right stick is moved up.
FortSetting.EditModeAimAssist_Description: Turns edit mode aim assist on/off. When on, this attracts your controller aim to the closest editing square in building edit mode.
FortSetting.TouchEdit_Description: Turns edit selection with touch on/off.
FortSetting.InvertPitchForFightersGamepad_Descriptio: Turns fighter controller steering inversion on/off. When on, moving the controller forward will pitch the fighter down.
FortSetting.InvertPitchForFightersMouse_Description: Turns fighter mouse steering inversion on/off. When on, moving the mouse forward will pitch the fighter down.
FortSetting.InvertPitchForFightersTouch_Description: Turns fighter touch steering inversion on/off. When on, moving touch forward will pitch the fighter down.
FortSetting.BackgroundAudio_Description: Turns game audio on/off when the game is in the background. When on, the game audio will continue to play when the game is minimized, or another window is focused.
FortSetting.ShowGlobalChat_Description: Turns global chat on/off. When on, you will see and be able to send messages in global chat.
FortSetting.ReplayHighQualityRecording_Description: Turns high quality replay recording on/off. When on, causes replays to record data more frequently, and disables some other optimizations which can cause visual issues during playback. Recording high quality replays can reduce performance and take up hard drive space.
FortSetting.InvertMouse_JoyConTwo_Description: Turns mouse controls look inversion on/off.  When on, your character will look down when the Joy‑Con 2 controller(s) is moved up.
FortSetting.InvertMouse_Description: Turns mouse look inversion on/off.  When on, your character will look down when the control is moved up.
FortSetting.CreativeTurboDelete_Description: Turns on/off the ability to rapidly delete things in creative mode by holding down the delete button.
FortSetting.PeripheralLighting_Description: Turns peripheral lighting on/off for supported devices.
FortSetting.ReplayRecordingCreativeMode_Description: Turns replay recording on/off for Creative Mode matches. Recording creative replays can reduce performance and take up hard drive space.
FortSetting.ReplayRecordingLargeTeams_Description: Turns replay recording on/off for large team modes. When on, replays are recorded and stored after each match with large teams, such as 50v50 or Teams of 20. Recording large team replays can reduce performance and take up hard drive space.
FortSetting.ReplayRecording_Description: Turns replay recording on/off. When on, replays are recorded and stored after each match. Recording replays can reduce performance and take up hard drive space.
FortSetting.EquipFirstBuildingPieceWhenSwappingQuick: Turns reset building choice on/off. When on, switching to build mode will always bring up the first build piece. When off, the game will remember and bring up the last build piece you were using.
Turns reset building choice on/off. When on, switching to build mode will always bring up the first build piece. When off, the game will remember and bring up the last build piece you were using.
FortSetting.ShowSpectatorCount_Description: Turns spectator count on/off. When on, a count of players spectating you is shown on the in-game HUD in the lower left.
FortSetting.TapToInteract_Description: Turns tap to search/interact on/off. When on, search/interact will require a single press. When off, the search/interact must be held for the duration of the timer.
FortSetting.GamepadVibration_Description: Turns the controller's vibration feature on/off.
FortSetting.ReplayRecordingIndicator_Description: Turns the recording indicator on/off. When on, a red dot and REC text will pop up at the top right corner of the screen whenever your match is being recorded.
FortSetting.VisualizeAudioSources_Description: Turns the visual radial indicator for some sounds on/off.  When on, you will see the visual radial indicator for some sounds, but in game sound will no longer pan or provide spatialization. Creators may set this to OFF for their island experience, which will override your selection.
FortSetting.VisualizeAudioSourcesWithSpat_Descriptio: Turns the visual radial indicator for some sounds on/off.  When on, you will see the visual radial indicator for some sounds. Creators may set this to OFF for their island experience, which will override your selection.
FortSetting.InvertTouch_Description: Turns touch look inversion on/off. When on, your character will look down when the control is moved up.
FortSetting.TurboBuild_Description: Turns turbo building on/off. When on, holding down the build button will automatically continue to build the selected piece when looking around. Turning off will set each building to require one press.
FortSetting.TSR_Description:
Unreal Engine's Temporal Super Resolution displays similarily detailed images with reduced load on your GPU for each frame. When enabled, it allows you to prioritize performance or quality.

Recommended (default): Selects the 3D resolution based what works best for your display resolution.

Performance: Prioritise frame rate over final image quality by rendering at lower resolution.

Balanced: Balances the image quality versus performance.

Quality: Prioritise final image quality over performance by rendering at higher resolution.

Native: Allows you to render frames at the native display resolution at the expense of performance.

Custom: Allows you to customize the 3D resolution on a slider.
FortSetting.TriggerHapticsUseThreshold_Description: Use Adaptive Trigger effect to trigger button presses. Disabling this will use the default trigger thresholds to detect button presses.
FortSetting.EnableRudderControl_Description: Use a foot controller for inputs if connected.
FortSetting.MoveStickAccelerates_Description_Switch: Using the Left Stick accelerates or brakes the vehicle you're driving. When disabled, the Left Stick is only for steering while all accelerating and braking is handled by the ZL/ZR Buttons. This option only affects vehicles where you can accelerate and brake with the ZL/ZR Buttons.
FortSetting.MoveStickAccelerates_Description_Switch2: Using the Left Stick accelerates or brakes the vehicle you're driving. When disabled, the Left Stick is only for steering while all accelerating and braking is handled by the ZL/ZR Buttons. This option only affects vehicles where you can accelerate and brake with the ZL/ZR Buttons.
FortSetting.MoveStickAccelerates_Description_PS4: Using the left stick accelerates or brakes the vehicle you're driving. When disabled, the left stick is only for steering while all accelerating and braking is handled by the L2 & R2 buttons. This option only affects vehicles where you can accelerate and brake with the L2 & R2 buttons.
FortSetting.MoveStickAccelerates_Description_PS5: Using the left stick accelerates or brakes the vehicle you're driving. When disabled, the left stick is only for steering while all accelerating and braking is handled by the L2 & R2 buttons. This option only affects vehicles where you can accelerate and brake with the L2 & R2 buttons.
FortSetting.MoveStickAccelerates_Description: Using the left stick accelerates or brakes the vehicle you're driving. When disabled, the left stick is only for steering while all accelerating and braking is handled by the controller triggers. This option only affects vehicles where you can accelerate and brake with the triggers.
FortSetting.MoveStickAccelerates_Description_XB1: Using the left stick accelerates or brakes the vehicle you're driving. When disabled, the left stick is only for steering while all accelerating and braking is handled by the controller triggers. This option only affects vehicles where you can accelerate and brake with the triggers.
FortSetting.MoveStickAccelerates_Description_XSX: Using the left stick accelerates or brakes the vehicle you're driving. This option only affects vehicles where you can accelerate and brake with the triggers.
FortSetting.ColorBlindStrength_Description: Using the provided images, adjust the strength of the filter until you see the color correction that works best for you. There is a llama shape in the middle of each circle.
FortSetting.ColorBlindMode_Description: Using the provided images, test out the different color blind modes of the filter until you see the color correction that works best for you. There is a llama shape in the middle of each circle.
FortSetting.VSyncDisabledBecauseOfFrameGeneration: VSync must be disabled when NVIDIA DLSS Frame Generation is enabled.
FortSetting.TouchVerticalSensitivity_Description: Vertical Sensitivity Multiplier for Touch Input.
FortSetting.ViewDistance_Description: View distance determines how far away objects are culled for performance.
FortSetting.TimeReportsActionDescription: View how much time your child has spent in Fortnite, Creative, and Unreal Editor for Fortnite (UEFN) each week.
FortSetting.ReticleColorForEnemy_Description: What color the reticle is when an enemy is in range
FortSetting.QuickWeaponToggleFunction_Description: What should happen when you tap Quick Weapon if your primary item is already selected? Toggling between slots 1 and 2 or your most recently equipped item can be helpful for quickly moving back and forth between your favorite two items.
FortSetting.ADSManualReloadResponse_Description: When ADS and manually reloading, choose whether you exit ADS or stay in ADS.
FortSetting.QuickEdit_Description_Switch:
When Simple Edit is on, you can edit a building piece by pressing the <strong>Edit</> Button once.

Changing the position of your crosshair when looking at a building piece will change the shape of your edit. When pressing <strong>Edit</> on an already edited building piece, it will be reset to its original shape.

Some edits in this mode are unavailable.

FortSetting.STW_ConsoleUnlockedFPS_Description: When on, maximizes framerate but will lead to inconsistent performance and higher input lag.
FortSetting.FollowCamera_Description_Switch: When on, moving left or right also turns the camera at the same time as long as you're not aiming or firing. FortSetting.EasyLootGridEnabled_Warning: When the Easy Loot widget is visible, it will disable other modes of interaction.
FortSetting.ResolutionForcedNative: When the Window Mode is set to <strong>Windowed Fullscreen</>, the resolution must match the native desktop resolution.
FortSetting.ReticleRecoilPullBehavior_Description: When the reticle is moved away from screen center by weapons featuring Offset Recoil, aiming in the opposite direction pulls aim back to center before turning the camera.
FortSetting.CameraResetAxes_Description_Switch:
Which axes get reset by the Reset Camera button?

If PITCH or BOTH are selected, pressing the Reset Camera button will bring the camera back to looking at the horizon.

If YAW or BOTH are selected, pressing the Reset Camera button will flick the camera to face the direction the Left Stick is pointing. For example, quickly look behind you by moving backwards and pressing the Reset Camera button. If the Left Stick is not engaged, the Reset Camera button will have no effect on the camera's yaw.

Which axes get reset by the Reset Camera button?

For example, quickly look behind you by moving backwards and pressing the Reset Camera button. If the Left Stick is not engaged, the Reset Camera button will have no effect on the camera's yaw.

FortSetting.TextChatRTR_Description:
While using Fortnite's text chat with text reporting on, players will be able to send the contents of their text chat channels to Epic Games for review of potential violations of our Community Rules.

You can see if text reporting is currently on or off in the text input field before typing a message.

<strong>OFF WHEN POSSIBLE:</> Text reporting will be off in direct messages and Party Channels where all players have text reporting OFF WHEN POSSIBLE.

<strong>ALWAYS ON:</> Text reporting will be on in every text chat conversation.

Note that text reporting will be on, regardless of your settings, in Party Channels when at least one player has text reporting set to ALWAYS ON, or when a player is added through Looking For Party, as well as in all Game Channels.{0}

Text reporting is <strong>ALWAYS ON</> for players under 18.

Learn more about text reporting here: https://fn.gg/voice-text-reporting
FortSetting.VoiceChatRVR_Description:
While using Fortnite's voice chat with voice reporting on, each participant's device will capture and store voice chat audio on a rolling five - minute basis, which will be sent to Epic Games only if a participant reports a chat violation to help us evaluate the report.

You can see if voice reporting is currently on or off in the Sidebar.

<strong>ALWAYS ON</> : Voice reporting will be on in every voice chat conversation.

<strong>OFF WHEN POSSIBLE</> : Voice reporting will be off in parties formed among friends where all players have voice reporting OFF WHEN POSSIBLE.

Voice reporting will be on if party chats include a player with voice reporting ALWAYS ON or a player added through Looking for Party.Voice reporting will also be on in all game channels.
Learn more about Voice Reporting here : {0} 

Voice reporting is always on for players under 18.
FortSetting.EpicAccountID_Description: Your Epic Account ID. Use this when contacting customer service
FortSetting.RequirePinToAddEpicFriends_Desc:

FortSettingValueDiscreteSupervised.GRAC_SETTING_DESCRIPTION:
Set the content rating for all devices using this account. Experiences rated higher than the selected rating will require PIN to be unlocked.

<strong>12+</>: The content of the game has no representation of anti-societal idea, distortion or profanity of religion and public morals that would be harmful to children under 12 emotionally and physically. The content and motif of the game has no representation of obscenity, violence, reproduction of gambling spirit that would be harmful to children under 12 (slight level of sexuality, violence, improper language(expletives), etc.).

<strong>All ages</>: The content of the game offers educational, or cultivating moral character and sentiments for children. The content and motif of the game have no representation of obscenity, violence, or reproduction of gambling spirit that harms children at all.

Learn more about ratings: https://www.grac.or.kr/english/enforcement/ratingguide.aspx
FortSettingValueDiscreteSupervised.Russia_SETTING_DESCRIPTION:
Set the content rating for all devices using this account. Experiences rated higher than the selected rating will require PIN to be unlocked.

<strong>12+</>: These games feature much more of a competitive edge. Game scenarios are set within a historical, futuristic or mythical fairy-tale context, enabling players to distance themselves sufficiently from events. This categorisation includes arcade games, strategy games and role-playing games as well as some military simulations.

<strong>6+</>: These games mostly involve family-friendly games which may be more exciting and competitive (e.g. via faster game speed and more complex tasks), such as racers, simulations, platforms and role-playing games.

<strong>All ages</>: The games do not include depictions of violence and do not confront children with any situations producing sustained anxiety.The atmosphere of children’s games often features friendly and colourful graphics. The more relaxed structure of the game does not put even young children under too much pressure to act. Game tasks are also appropriate for children.

Learn more about ratings: https://www.globalratings.com/ratings-guide-russia
FortSettingValueDiscreteSupervised.IARC_SETTING_DESCRIPTION:
Set the content rating for all devices using this account. Experiences rated higher than the selected rating will require PIN to be unlocked.

<strong>12+</>: Violence involving fantasy characters and/or non-graphic violence involving human-looking characters or animals is permitted. Non-graphic nudity, mild language and simulated gambling are also permitted, but sexual expletives are not.

<strong>7+</>: May contain some scenes or sounds that are frightening for children. Mild violence (implied or non-realistic) is permitted.

<strong>3+</>: Suitable for all age groups. Some violence in a comical or fantasy context is acceptable. Bad language is not permitted.

Learn more about ratings: https://www.globalratings.com/ratings-guide-generic
FortSettingValueDiscreteSupervised.FPB_SETTING_DESCRIPTION:
Set the content rating for all devices using this account. Experiences rated higher than the selected rating will require PIN to be unlocked.

<strong>13</>: Only persons aged 13 and above can play. Moderate in impact.

<strong>10-12 PG</>: Children aged 10 to 12 must have an adult present. Moderate in impact.

<strong>7-9 PG</>: Children aged 7 to 9 must have an adult present. Mild in impact.

<strong>PG</>: Restricted for children under the age of 7-years old. Children younger than 7 must have an adult present. This is the lowest rating given to home video releases/video games. Low in impact.

Learn more about ratings: https://www.fpb.org.za/ratings/
FortSettingValueDiscreteSupervised.ClassInd_SETTING_DESCRIPTION:
Set the content rating for all devices using this account. Experiences rated higher than the selected rating will require PIN to be unlocked.

<strong>16 years</>: The content has a moderate impact. Strong and frequent violence, mutilation, torture, sexual relations and/or use of illicit drugs may appear.

<strong>14 years</>: The content has a moderate impact. Deaths, eroticization, nudity, and/or insinuations of the use of illicit drugs may appear.

<strong>12 years</>: The content has a light impact. Swearing, sexual language, injuries, blood fights and/or alcohol and cigarette consumption may appear.

<strong>10 years</>: The content may have some impact, especially on young children. Light swearing, weapons, bloodless fights, horror scenes, references to alcohol and cigarettes and/or medicinal use of illicit drugs may appear.

<strong>6 years</>: The content may have a mild impact. Moderate sadness, mildly offensive language, fantasy violence, and educational content about sexuality and drugs may appear.

<strong>All ages</>: The content is suitable for all ages. May contain mild themes such as cartoon violence.

Learn more about ratings: https://www.globalratings.com/ratings-guide-classind
FortSettingValueDiscreteSupervised.USK_SETTING_DESCRIPTION:
Set the content rating for all devices using this account. Experiences rated higher than the selected rating will require PIN to be unlocked.

<strong>16+</>: Games approved for children aged 16 and above may include acts of violence.

<strong>12+</>: These games feature much more of a competitive edge. Game scenarios contain little violence, enabling players to distance themselves sufficiently from events.

<strong>6+</>: These games mostly involve family-friendly games which may be more exciting and competitive (e.g. via faster game speeds and more complex tasks).

<strong>Everyone</>: Games without age restriction are games which are directly aimed at children and young persons as well as at an adult buyer group.

Learn more about ratings: https://www.globalratings.com/ratings-guide-usk
FortSettingValueDiscreteSupervised.IGRS_SETTING_DESCRIPTION:
Set the content rating for all devices using this account. Experiences rated higher than the selected rating will require PIN to be unlocked.

<strong>Age Rating 3 Years Above</>: Game content suitable for ages 3 and above (3+) does not display references to cigarettes, alcohol, drugs, violence, blood, mutilation, cannibalism, coarse language, swearing, adult humor, nudity, pornography, gambling, or horror, and does not include online interaction features such as conversations.

<strong>Age Rating 7 Years Above</>: Game content suitable for ages 7 and above (7+) does not display references to cigarettes, alcohol, drugs, violence, mutilation, cannibalism, or unrealistic blood elements, does not use coarse language, swearing, or adult humor, does not show nudity, pornography, gambling, or horror, and does not include online interaction features such as conversations.

<strong>Age Rating 13 Years Above</>: Game content suitable for ages 13 and above (13+) does not display references to cigarettes, alcohol, or drugs; human mutilation or cannibalism (though blood elements may appear); adult humor with sexual connotations; nudity or pornography; gambling or intense horror, and may include limited non-hateful animated violence with unrealistic weapons and filtered online conversations.

Learn more about ratings: https://globalratings.com/ratings-definitions
FortSettingValueDiscreteSupervised.GMedia_SETTING_DESCRIPTION:
Set the content rating for all devices using this account. Experiences rated higher than the selected rating will require PIN to be unlocked.

<strong>Games suitable for ages 3 and above</>: Games appropriate for all ages, such as sports games. Some games may contain violence, but in a cartoonish context suitable for children.

<strong>Games suitable for ages 7 and above</>: Games that may contain some frightening scenes or sounds, such as implied violence, or characters interacting with violence in an unrealistic way (for example, characters disappearing after they die).

<strong>Games suitable for ages 12 and above</>: Games that may contain scary sounds or frightening visual effects, such as showing minor details of injuries.

<strong>Games suitable for ages 16 and above</>: Games that contain realistic violence against humans or human-like creatures, including portraying criminal acts positively or without negative consequences, in addition to the use of inappropriate language.

Learn more about ratings: https://globalratings.com/ratings-definitions
FortSettingValueDiscreteSupervised.DGSC_SETTING_DESCRIPTION:
Set the content rating for all devices using this account. Experiences rated higher than the selected rating will require PIN to be unlocked.

<strong>General Audience</>: Suitable for all ages.

<strong>Protected (6+)</>: Suitable for ages 6 and above.

<strong>Parental Guidance 12+</>: Suitable for ages 12 and above.

Learn more about ratings: https://globalratings.com/ratings-definitions
FortSettingValueDiscreteSupervised.ACB_SETTING_DESCRIPTION:
Set the content rating for all devices using this account. Experiences rated higher than the selected rating will require PIN to be unlocked.

<strong>Mature</>: Films and computer games classified M (Mature) contain content of a moderate impact. M-rated films and computer games are not recommended for children under the age of 15. They include portrayals of elements such as violence and themes that require a mature outlook. However, children under the age of 15 may legally access this material as the classification rating is an advisory category.

<strong>Parental Guidance</>: The impact of PG classified films and computer games should be no higher than mild, but they may contain content that children find confusing or upsetting and may require the guidance of parents, teachers or guardians. For example, they may contain classifiable elements such as language and themes that are mild in impact.

<strong>General</>: Is suitable for everyone. General products may contain classifiable elements such as language and themes that are very mild in impact

Learn more about ratings: https://www.globalratings.com/ratings-guide-acb
FortSettingValueDiscreteSupervised.PEGI_SETTING_DESCRIPTION:
Set the content rating for all devices using this account. Experiences rated higher than the selected rating will require PIN to be unlocked.

<strong>PEGI 12 (age 12+)</>: Violence towards fantasy characters or non-realistic violence towards human-like characters. Sexual innuendo, mild bad language and/or gambling.

<strong>PEGI 7 (age 7+)</>: Mild forms of violence and scenes that younger children may find frightening.

<strong>PEGI 3 (age 3+)</>: Considered suitable for all age groups.

Learn more about ratings: https://www.globalratings.com/ratings-guide-pegi
FortSettingValueDiscreteSupervised.ESRB_SETTING_DESCRIPTION:
Set the content rating for all devices using this account. Experiences rated higher than the selected rating will require PIN to be unlocked.

<strong>Teen</>: Content is generally suitable for ages 13 and up. May contain violence, suggestive themes, crude humor, minimal blood, simulated gambling and/or infrequent use of strong language.

<strong>Everyone 10+</>: Content is generally suitable for ages 10 and up. May contain more cartoon, fantasy or mild violence, mild language and/or minimal suggestive themes.

<strong>Everyone</>: Content is generally suitable for all ages. may contain minimal cartoon, fantasy or mild violence and/or infrequent use of mild language.

Learn more about ratings: https://www.globalratings.com/ratings-guide-esrb
FortSettingValueDiscreteSupervised.VoiceChat_ExpandedSettings_Description:
Sets who you can speak with in groups, parties, and games.

<strong>Everybody</>: You can voice chat with everyone.

<strong>Friends, team members, and connections</>: You can voice chat with: Epic friends, platform friends{0}, connections, and everyone on your team. Connections are people who aren’t your direct friends but are linked to you through your friends. Team sizes vary by experience and can range from a few players to everyone in a game.

<strong>Friends Only</>: You can voice chat only with your Epic and platform friends.

<strong>Off</>: You can't voice chat with anyone.

<strong>About voice reporting</>
Voice reporting stores chat audio on each participant’s device on a rolling five-minute basis. This audio will be sent to Epic Games if someone reports a violation. Voice reporting is usually on, except if all in a chat has reporting off. You can check if voice reporting is on or off in the sidebar. Voice reporting is always on for under 18s.
FortSettingValueDiscreteSupervised.VoiceChat_ExpandedSettingsWithDeprecated:
Sets who you can speak with in groups, parties, and games.

<strong>Everybody</>: You can voice chat with everyone.

<strong>Friends, team members, and connections</>: You can voice chat with: Epic friends, platform friends{0}, connections, and everyone on your team. Connections are people who aren’t your direct friends but are linked to you through your friends. Team sizes vary by experience and can range from a few players to everyone in a game.

<strong>Friends and Teammates</>: You can voice chat with your Epic and platform friends and with others who are not your friends in parties and teams. Team sizes vary by experience, from a few players to all in a game.

<strong>Friends Only</>: You can voice chat only with your Epic and platform friends.

<strong>Off</>: You can't voice chat with anyone.

<strong>About voice reporting</>
Voice reporting stores chat audio on each participant’s device on a rolling five-minute basis. This audio will be sent to Epic Games if someone reports a violation. Voice reporting is usually on, except if all in a chat has reporting off. You can check if voice reporting is on or off in the sidebar. Voice reporting is always on for under 18s.
FortSettingValueDiscreteSupervised.TextChat_ExpandedSettings_Description:
Sets who you can text chat with in groups, parties, and games. You can only send and receive one-on-one messages with your friends.

{OptionWarning}<strong>Everybody</>: You can text chat with everyone. 

<strong>Friends, team members, and connections</>: You can text chat with: Epic friends, platform friends{Platform}, connections, and everyone on your team. Connections are people who aren’t your direct friends but are linked to you through your friends. Team sizes vary by experience and can range from a few players to everyone in a game.

{OptionDesc}<strong>Friends Only</>: You can text chat only with your Epic and platform friends. 

<strong>Off</>: You can't text chat with anyone.

<strong>About Text Reporting</>
Text reporting enables the contents of your text chats to be sent to Epic Games when someone reports a violation.
Text reporting is usually on, except if everyone in a chat has it turned off. You can check if text reporting is on or off in the message box.
Text reporting is always on for under 18s and in all game chats. In those chats, our systems will automatically detect and report threats of real-world violence, child endangerment, and self-harm to Epic for review.

FortSocialProfileSettingRegistry.SocialPrivacy_Region_Description:
Controls visibility of your matchmaking region to other players.

<strong>PRIVATE</>: No one can see your matchmaking region.

<strong>FRIENDS ONLY</>: Only friends can see your matchmaking region.

<strong>PUBLIC</>: Anyone can see your matchmaking region.
FortSocialProfileSettingRegistry.SocialPrivacy_MutualFriends_Description:
Controls visibility of your mutual friends to other players.

<strong>PRIVATE</>: No one can see your mutual friends.

<strong>FRIENDS ONLY</>: Only friends can see your mutual friends.

<strong>PUBLIC</>: Anyone can see your mutual friends.
FortSocialProfileSettingRegistry.SocialStaus_Online_Description:
Controls visibility of your online status to other players.

<strong>ONLINE</>: Other players see you as online.

<strong>AWAY</>: Other players see you as away.
FortSocialProfileSettingRegistry.SocialPrivacy_Badges_Description:
Controls visibility of your passes to other players.

<strong>PRIVATE</>: No one can see your passes.

<strong>FRIENDS ONLY</>: Only friends can see your passes.

<strong>PUBLIC</>: Anyone can see your passes.
FortSocialProfileSettingRegistry.SocialPrivacy_Language_Description:
Controls visibility of your preferred language to other players.

<strong>PRIVATE</>: No one can see your language.

<strong>FRIENDS ONLY</>: Only friends can see your language.

<strong>PUBLIC</>: Anyone can see your language.
FortSocialProfileSettingRegistry.SocialStaus_GameReadiness_Description:
Controls whether you are playing or sitting out.

<strong>PLAYING</>: You will play the next match.

<strong>SITTING OUT</>: You will not play the next match.
FortSocialProfileSettingRegistry.SocialStaus_Lfg_Description:
Controls whether you are visible to other players looking for a party.

<strong>ON</>: You are discoverable by other players looking for a party.

<strong>OFF</>: You are not discoverable by other players looking for a party.
FortSocialProfileSettingRegistry.SocialPrivacy_ShareActivity_Description:
Share Activity allows you to share your play history and online presence with the Fortnite community.

ON: When turned on, your online presence will be publicly visible and the games that you are playing will be posted to your Creator page under the Shared Play History section.

OFF: When turned off, your online presence will not be publicly visible. Also, gameplay that occurs when this option is off will not be posted publicly to your Creator Page.
FortSocialProfileSettingRegistry.SocialStatus_PartyJoinability_Descriptio:
This setting is for how players join your party.

<strong>Invite Only</>: You or someone in your party have to invite players or accept their requests to join in order for them to join the party.

<strong>FRIENDS</>: Players on your friends list and friends of party members will be able to join your party at any time.

NOTE: A party can always be joined via invite.

FortSocialToolkit.VoiceChatVRR_Accept_U18_NewPlayer:
Voice reporting helps us review reported violations of our Community Rules.

• When voice chat is turned on, this feature stores voice chat audio on each participant's device on a rolling five-minute basis, which will be sent to Epic Games only if a participant reports a violation.

• Voice reporting is always on if you're under 18.

See your voice chat settings for more details and options.
FortSocialToolkit.VoiceChatVRR_Accept_O18_NewPlayer:
Voice reporting helps us review reported violations of our Community Rules.

• When voice chat is turned on, this feature stores voice chat audio on each participant's device on a rolling five-minute basis, which will be sent to Epic Games only if a participant reports a violation.

• Voice reporting is usually on, except in parties formed among friends where all players prefer voice reporting off.

• You can see if voice reporting is currently on or off in the Sidebar.

See your voice chat settings for more details and options.

IslandTagInternal.sponsored_row_info: Developers pay to promote their islands in the Sponsored row. The placement of islands in the Sponsored row is influenced by a bidding process, which is based on the amount the developer bids and the number of developers involved.
IslandTagInternal.algorithmic_info: This content is shown based upon Discover metrics such as what's popular and what's trending. This content can change at any time. This is not a personalized recommendation.
IslandTagInternal.socialrecommendations_info:
This island is being recommended to you based on the play history of your friends and players you recently played with.

 If you want to, you can opt-out of receiving personalized recommendations in Settings by modifying the "Allow Personalization of Discover Recommendations" setting under Account and Privacy->Gameplay Privacy. If you opt-out, you will continue to see recommendations, but they will not be personalized.
IslandTagInternal.friendrecommendations_info:
This island is being recommended to you based on what your friends have recommended.

 Should you want to, you can opt-out of receiving personalized recommendations in Settings by modifying the "Allow Personalization of Discover Recommendations" setting under Account and Privacy->Gameplay Privacy. If you opt-out, you will continue to see recommendations, but they will not be personalized.
IslandTagInternal.newrecommendations_info:
This island is being recommended to you based on your recently played experiences. It considers your recent play history and the play history of other opted-in users to recommend content to you.

 If you want to, you can opt-out of receiving personalized recommendations in Settings by modifying the "Allow Personalization of Discover Recommendations" setting under Account and Privacy->Gameplay Privacy. If you opt-out, you will continue to see recommendations, but they will not be personalized.
IslandTagInternal.recommendations_info:
This island is being recommended to you based on your recently played experiences. It considers your recent play history and the play history of other opted-in users to recommend content to you.

 If you want to, you can opt-out of receiving personalized recommendations in Settings by modifying the "Allow Personalization of Discover Recommendations" setting under Account and Privacy->Gameplay Privacy. If you opt-out, you will continue to see recommendations, but they will not be personalized.
IslandTagInternal.interests_info:
This island is being recommended to you because you asked to see more games like your favorited Collections, Islands, and Creators. It does not consider your play history or social interactions.

 If you want to, you can favorite Islands, Creators, and Collections in Discover and unfavorite Islands, Creators, and Collections in your Library.

UFortAnimGraphNode_AimOffsets.MenuDescTooltip:
Applies an aim offset blend space to the input pose, sampled at evaluate time.
Supports optional bone-compensated yaw and pitch correction.
UFortAnimGraphNode_AimOffsets.AimOffsetsTooltip:
Applies an aim offset blend space to the input pose.
The blend space is sampled at evaluate time so that an optional compensation
can be applied using any bone's component-space rotation.

UFortAnimGraphNode_CachedSequenceEvaluat.Title: Cached Sequence Evaluator
UFortAnimGraphNode_CachedSequenceEvaluat.Tooltip:
Evaluates a frame in an animation sequence. When CacheName is set, the extracted
pose is stored and shared across all nodes in the same ABP instance
 — including linked graphs and self layers — that use the same CacheName

Without a CacheName, the pose is evaluated each frame without caching.

UserOptionDefinition.Device_Floor_SweepTrigger_C.StartSequenc: Activates the sequencer from the beginning on receiving a signal from the selected channel.
UserOptionDefinition.CP_P_Atmospheric_SmokeFloor_C.Opacity.De: Changes how opaque the smoke surface is. Lower numbers mean the smoke will draw more transparently.
UserOptionDefinition.CP_P_Atmospheric_SmokeFloor_C.Smoke Tili: Changes the amount of detail on the smoke surface.
UserOptionDefinition.CP_P_Atmospheric_SmokeFloor_C.Smoke Colo: Changes the color of the smoke.
UserOptionDefinition.CP_P_Atmospheric_SmokeFloor_C.Motion Spe: Changes the speed at which the smoke moves (pans) across the surface.
UserOptionDefinition.CP_P_Atmospheric_SmokeFloor_C.Motion Typ: Changes the way smoke moves across the surface.
UserOptionDefinition.Trap_Floor_Minigame_Spawner_C.Cycle to N: Cycle to the next item in the Spawner's inventory on receiving a signal from the selected channel.
Determines how accurately the Sentry will target players in range.
UserOptionDefinition.Trap_Floor_Backup_C.RespawnPresetIndex.D: Determines how long the Sentry will wait before respawning after being eliminated.
UserOptionDefinition.Trap_Floor_Backup_C.HealthPresetIndex.De: Determines how much health the Sentry will spawn with.
Determines if the Sentry can target Fiends and Wildlife that are on another team than the Sentry. If Friendly Team is set to NONE, then the Sentry will only target Wildlife if they are tamed by a Player.
UserOptionDefinition.CP_P_Atmospheric_SmokeFloor_C.DoubleSide: Determines if the smoke will render when viewed from underneath. Keep note that using this option makes the material more expensive to render, which will reduce performance when used excessively.
UserOptionDefinition.Device_Floor_SweepTrigger_C.UseFlashingW: Determines if the warning signs displayed when the sequencer damages players flash.
UserOptionDefinition.CP_P_Atmospheric_Aesthetic_C.Enabled On: Determines the game phases during which the device will be enabled. Pre-Game includes all phases prior to the Game starting (the waiting for players lobby on Featured Islands and the Game Start Countdown).
UserOptionDefinition.CP_P_Atmospheric_SmokeFloor_C.Enabled On: Determines the game phases during which the device will be enabled. Pre-Game includes all phases prior to the Game starting (the waiting for players lobby on Featured Islands and the Game Start Countdown).
UserOptionDefinition.Trap_Floor_Backup_C.RangePresetIndex.Des: Determines the range at which the Sentry will detect players.
UserOptionDefinition.Trap_Floor_Backup_C.ShieldPresetIndex.De: Determines the shield level that the Sentry will spawn with.
UserOptionDefinition.Trap_Floor_Backup_C.WeaponPresetIndex.De: Determines the type of weapon the Sentry will spawn with.
UserOptionDefinition.Trap_Floor_Backup_C.TargetPresetIndex.De: Determines whether the Sentry will only shoot at players to which it has line of sight, or whether it will attempt to shoot at any player in range regardless of visibility.
UserOptionDefinition.Trap_Floor_Backup_C.bSpawnOnMinigameStar: Determines whether the Sentry will spawn as soon as the game starts.
Determines whether the Sentry's aim improves the longer it targets a player.
UserOptionDefinition.Trap_Floor_Backup_C.bAwardElimination.De: Determines whether the player is awarded an elimination for destroying the Sentry.
UserOptionDefinition.Trap_Floor_Backup_C.TeamPresetIndex.Desc: Determines which team the Sentry will treat as friendly.
UserOptionDefinition.Trap_Floor_Minigame_Spawner_C.Disable.De: Disable this Spawner on receiving a signal from the selected channel.
UserOptionDefinition.Trap_Floor_Minigame_Spawner_C.Enable.Des: Enable this Spawner on receiving a signal from the selected channel.
UserOptionDefinition.Device_Floor_SweepTrigger_C.ResumeSequen: Resumes the sequencer from the last position it was stopped on receiving a signal from the selected channel.
UserOptionDefinition.Trap_Floor_Backup_C.OverrideEliminationS: Sets the amount of score awarded to the player for eliminating the Sentry.
UserOptionDefinition.Trap_Floor_Backup_C.Show Range Visualiza: Should the Range Visualization Sphere be visible while editing the device.
UserOptionDefinition.Trap_Floor_Minigame_Spawner_C.Spawn Item: Spawn the next item on receiving a signal from the selected channel.
UserOptionDefinition.Device_Floor_SweepTrigger_C.StopSequence: Stops the sequencer on receiving a signal from the selected channel.
